improvements.adversarial_note: AGAINST: w['percussion'] being hand-set is a FEATURE not a bug if Jordan wants strike_concentration (beak vs flat) folded in — p_auth(mass,pob_frac) has no concentration term, so a war-pick and a flat mace of equal mass/balance would defeat armour identically, which is physically wrong (the pick concentrates force). The CLEANER fix is to multiply p_auth by the already-baked-but-dead geo['perc_conc'] (geometry.py:48-51) — which kills two birds (WP-1 + WP-3). So WP-1 alone may regress pick-vs-mace; do WP-1 AND WP-3 together or not at all.

improvements.what: Decategoricalise NON-blunt heft and the wt='heavy' cliff. Replace the 2-value HEFT={'light':0,'heavy':3} (core.py:48) and the wt=='heavy' booleans (WEIGHT_PEN systems.py:23, ACT_WEIGHT systems.py:48, D_WT systems.py:58) with a continuous mass/MoI term.
improvements.mechanism: core.damage heft (core.py:82 else-branch HEFT.get(weapon_wt,0)) -> a continuous f(mass) e.g. clamp(K*(mass-1.0),0,3); systems.weapon_tempo pen (systems.py:23), act_cost (systems.py:48), str_demand (systems.py:58) read mass instead of (wt=='heavy'). IN: mass (already in WEAPONS, already consumed by p_auth). resolver: damage/tempo/cost/str_demand. OUT: heft, tempo penalty, action cost, str demand. This is the corpus's core proposal MINUS the MoI/pommel_kg machinery — mass alone removes the cliff.
improvements.ners_impact: S++ (the wt='heavy' boolean is a genuine cliff/seam: greatsword mass2.7 and longsword mass1.4 both = 'heavy' so pay identical WEIGHT_PEN/ACT_WEIGHT/D_WT despite ~2x mass; arming 1.2 and sabre 0.9 both 'light' = 0. P-iii says no cliffs — this is one). E+ (one number, mass, does the work of a category). N-neutral (no new lever — mass already exists). Smoothness gain is real and cheap.
improvements.adversarial_note: AGAINST: introduces K_HEFT/K_TEMPO/K_STRD/K_ACT scale gains (4 uncalibrated knobs) where today there are exact tuned constants (WEIGHT_PEN=0.8 etc.). Every gain is a calibration liability and the corpus itself flags them [SIM-CALIBRATE]. The cliff is also SMALL in practice — combat_analysis_rev2 §2 finds 'weight is NOT a primary driver' (mace tanks DESPITE heavy), so smoothing a non-dominant axis buys little churn. Mitigation: do mass-linear (one gain per site, calibrated to keep the current heavy-value at the current heavy-mass) NOT MoI — MoI needs pommel_kg and a 3-class branch (WP-5) for marginal extra realism.

improvements.what: Wire the dead baked geo coefficients (thrust/cut/perc_conc) into their natural consumers, OR delete them. They are computed every import (combatant.py:49-53 sets WEAPONS[w]['geo']=bake(...)) and never read.
improvements.mechanism: Grep confirms WEAPONS[w]['geo'], 'thrust', 'cut', 'perc_conc', 'halfsword' have ZERO read sites. Natural consumers: geo['perc_conc'] -> multiply core.p_auth or the blunt armour-defeat cap (WP-1) so a war-pick (strike_concentration 0.85->perc_conc~0.93) out-defeats a flat mace; geo['cut'] -> a cut-quality term in core.coupling shear delivery (currently DELIVERY is a flat per-head constant, geometry.py-derived cut keenness/curvature unused); geo['thrust'] -> redundant with gap (gap already = gap_precision(pc,cs)), so DELETE thrust. IN: baked geometry. resolver: p_auth/coupling. OUT: armour-defeat, cut damage.
improvements.ners_impact: N++ (dead-data elimination is the purest NERS-N win — either it feeds churn or it goes). Wiring perc_conc gives the pick/mace distinction churn (a meaningful weapon choice vs plate); wiring cut gives sabre/greatsword edge-quality churn. Deleting thrust removes a duplicate of gap (anti-lever-pile).
improvements.adversarial_note: AGAINST: wiring cut/perc_conc adds two more multiplicative terms to damage, each a calibration surface, and combat_analysis already shows the tier-list is reach-dominated (r=0.83) — finer damage granularity may be invisible under reach. The honest minimal action is DELETE the dead baked fields (and the bake() outputs for them) to stop paying import cost and remove the false 'available to modules' promise (combatant.py:53), and only wire perc_conc IF WP-1 proceeds (it's the fix that makes WP-1 physically correct). Wiring cut/thrust is speculative; gate on a sim showing the cut-quality spread changes any matchup.

improvements.what: Resolve the seize-lever DEAD-ABILITY drift. 'vorschlag' (german) and 'sen_no_sen' (JAPANESE flagship) target lever='seize' (tradition.py:96,106) which has NO consumer — Grep confirms no ability_bonus(_, 'seize')/ability_factor(_, 'seize') call exists in wrapper.py/systems.py; pre-contact seizure was CUT (wrapper.py:27, systems.py:243).
improvements.mechanism: Either (a) RE-POINT vorschlag/sen_no_sen at a live lever — e.g. seize-as-initiative: have them add to the INIT_STEAL_INDES path (wrapper.py:151) or seed initiative at engagement start via ability_bonus(c,'seize'); or (b) RETIRE both abilities + the 'seize' lever and fix ability_armature.md:106,131,167 which falsely marks them '✅ live'/'Live levers: seize'. IN: equipped abilities. resolver: initiative substrate. OUT: Vor edge.
improvements.ners_impact: N (today two abilities and a lever are pure dead weight — a tradition equips them and NOTHING changes, the worst churn failure: a choice with no path-branch. Japan's signature sen-no-sen literally cannot fire). E (the doc claiming them live is a legibility trap — a player/designer believes Japan has a working seize). Fixing it either restores the flagship mechanic's churn (option a) or removes the false affordance (option b).
improvements.adversarial_note: AGAINST option (a): re-adding pre-contact seizure was DELIBERATELY cut 2026-06-05 after ablation showed ~0 outcome impact (washed out by per-beat dynamics, wrapper.py:27). Re-pointing seize at initiative risks re-introducing the same wash-out — you'd be rebuilding a mechanic the engine already proved inert. The disciplined move is option (b): retire it and repair the doc. The corpus's analytical-ledger D-1 wants to REVIVE it ('wire precommit + seize, highest leverage') — but reviving a proven-inert lever is lever-pile dressed as a fix. Only revive if a NEW consumer (not the cut pre-contact contest) is designed AND simmed to non-zero impact.

improvements.what: Treat pommel_kg as OPTIONAL, not a required new primitive. The corpus adds pommel_kg (back-solved from sourced PoB) to make PoB exactly reproducible. But the engine already carries pob_frac directly.
improvements.mechanism: weapon_physics.derive (weapon_physics.py:33-59) needs pommel_kg only to COMPUTE PoB from mass; the engine already STORES pob_frac per weapon (combatant.py:14-27). For every engine output the corpus targets (heft via MoI, str_demand via static_moment=mass*PoB), PoB is available as pob_frac*length directly — no pommel needed. pommel_kg only matters if you compute PoB forward from materials, which the engine never does. IN: pob_frac (existing). resolver: any heft/MoI term. OUT: same. So: if WP-2 uses MoI, approximate MoI ~ mass*pob_frac*head_len^2 (the ledger line 60 formula) from EXISTING fields; skip pommel_kg.
improvements.ners_impact: N++ (refuses a new primitive when an existing one suffices — directly the 'an ADDED mechanic must itself be necessary' rule). E+ (no new per-weapon number for the designer to source/maintain). The corpus's own preliminary doc admits rho=0.69 vs the existing pob_frac and 3 named residuals — i.e. the physics back-solve does NOT cleanly beat the hand-set pob_frac it's trying to replace.
improvements.adversarial_note: AGAINST: pob_frac is hand-assigned and the calibration doc shows some values are off (longsword pob_frac 0.14 but the physics says rearward -0.09; staff 0.05 vs computed forward 0.12). If Jordan wants the pob_frac VALUES corrected to physical truth, the back-solve is the tool. But that's a one-time data-correction of an EXISTING field, NOT a reason to add pommel_kg to the live vector. Correct pob_frac offline using the physics; don't ship pommel_kg into combatant.py.

improvements.what: Fix the /home/claude sandbox imports so the engine is runnable on the clone / CI / Godot — prerequisite for ANY corpus §5 sim gate.
improvements.mechanism: core.py:3, combatant.py:4, systems.py:4, wrapper.py:4 do sys.path.insert(0,'/home/claude'...) then import r1_sigma_resolution/r8_parity_harness/m1_dice_sigma_core. Those files physically live at tests/sim/v32-combat-balance/. Replace the hardcoded sandbox paths with a path computed relative to __file__ pointing at the substrate dir (or vendor m1/r1/r8 into the engine dir). IN: none. OUT: import resolution. systems.py:46 also does a runtime 'import r8_parity_harness' inside stamina_max.
improvements.ners_impact: Not NERS-scored — it's a correctness/portability P1. But it GATES the whole corpus: the calibration doc §5 commit-gate (mirror=50, tier spread, no-one-shot) requires running the harness, which is impossible while the engine can't import its own substrate. The corpus's turnkey 'drop weapon_physics.py into the dir and run §5' fails immediately on import.
improvements.adversarial_note: AGAINST: if the canonical execution environment IS the sandbox (the engine was authored to run at /home/claude and is only mirrored into the repo for version control), then the paths are correct THERE and 'broken' only on this clone. But CLAUDE.md says 'the working tree is the source of truth' and there's a Godot companion repo (valoria-game) that must consume this — neither can use /home/claude. So this is a real P1 regardless; the sandbox-only assumption is the stale-rule the project explicitly retired.

reject: REJECT the K_REACH*fwd_extent_m reach rewrite (calibration §4 reach_base row). reach_base is already parametric and blade-length-grounded (r=+0.87, combat_analysis_rev2 §2); reach_adj is already the continuous per-weapon fine-tune. Swapping a tuned formula for K_REACH*fwd_extent_m where fwd_extent_m=head_len*UNIT_M would make reach track HEAD LENGTH alone — which mis-ranks: spear head_len=5.5 would dominate absurdly (the preliminary doc's OWN pass-1 diagnostic shows head_len is mostly shaft for poles, PoB 72cm 'wrong'). Net: a calibrated, validated reach model replaced by one the corpus admits needs the 3-class fix to not blow up. No NERS gain; pure regression risk.

REJECT adding all 5 K_* gains as live config. K_REACH/K_HEFT/K_TEMPO/K_STRD/K_ACT are flagged [SIM-CALIBRATE] / uncalibrated by the corpus itself (weapon_physics.py:27-31, calibration §4-5). Five new uncalibrated knobs replacing exact tuned constants is the canonical lever-pile defect — each gain is a calibration surface and a place for a cliff to hide. If WP-2 proceeds, introduce ONE mass-linear gain per site calibrated to preserve the current heavy-value, not the full MoI machinery.

REJECT the analytical-ledger's claim that 'traditions tune but never route' is a defect requiring the imposition gate (ledger §5b, D-5). It is accurate as DESCRIPTION (eff_cw multipliers re-weight sigma at ~9 sites, none branch the state-graph path) but the engine's whole design thesis (tradition.py:1-18) is that a tradition is a cognitive-mode BIAS over a shared substrate, NOT a separate rule-set — flat multipliers ARE the intended architecture (P-ii uniform-leverage; E one-number-does-more). Adding path-routing gates at 4 transitions is a NEW subsystem that fights the substrate philosophy. The churn is already there via the bias (a german fighter wins binds more often -> different paths emerge). Routing is lever-pile dressed as coverage.

REJECT instantiating the short-blunt weapon class / 2H pole-blade family / messer (ledger §5a, D-4) as a PHYSICS-corpus action. These are weapon-roster expansions (creative + balance calls), unrelated to the weapon-physics wiring, and combat_analysis shows the roster is already reach-saturated (adding short weapons = adding losers; mace/dagger already tank at 24.8%/26.8%). Out of scope for this reconciliation; defer to a roster-design pass with its own sim.

REJECT the ledger's 'pob_frac is recorded-but-inert / POB is inert' (ledger line 60-61). STALE: pob_frac is consumed by core.p_auth (core.py:38) for blunt heft. The ledger contradicts the calibration doc's own [CORRECTION]. Do not act on the inert-POB framing.

REJECT re-adding pre-contact seizure to revive vorschlag/sen_no_sen as-was (the ledger D-1 'revive seize' default). It was cut after ablation showed ~0 impact (wrapper.py:27). Reviving the identical mechanism rebuilds proven-inert code. See WP-4 option (b) as the disciplined alternative.
creative_layer: pommel_kg back-solved values (rapier 0.348, greatsword 0.548 heaviest) match weapon REPUTATION — if pob_frac is ever corrected from the physics, the per-weapon pommel feel ('a rapier's heavy pommel is why it balances at the hand') is flavour Jordan can surface in weapon descriptions even if it never enters the engine vector.

The 3-class split {bladed, hafted_tip, hafted_block} (preliminary §1) is a clean in-world weapon-smithing ontology — grip-plus-head vs full-shaft-plus-point vs haft-plus-lump — usable as the diegetic category a smith/merchant speaks in, orthogonal to the engine's head-shape axis.

The analytical-ledger's 6 Valoria-native traditions (Constraint/Tempo-read/Intent-seize/Flow/Root/Reach, ledger §3.1 Layer B) and their in-world naming (decision D-7) are explicitly reserved as Jordan-only — the channel-vectors are mechanical scaffold; the cultural names and lore are the creative layer. The ledger correctly refuses to name them.
Short-blunt-class flavour (shillelagh/canne/tonfa/sap, ledger §5a#1) as a low-status 'levy/improvised' weapon niche — a narrative role (the peasant's club, the bravo's sap) more than a balance need.

The 'Root' tradition concept (leverage 1.35 + strike, battlefield/clinch apex, ledger §3.2 Layer B) as the armour-defeat / half-sword / poleaxe school — a thematically rich 'the man who fights in the press, in harness' identity for the percussion weapons that currently have no tradition home (mace is orphaned).
reconcile.verdict: ADOPT-PARTIAL. The resolution spine the audit prompt centres on (mu-shift, sigma_N-scaled leverage core.py:31; ER-2 inline core.py:14-24; soft_cap 1.5*tanh, sigma_n 0.8*sqrt(pool)) is NERS-sound and the corpus correctly does not touch it. The corpus is most valuable as a DRIFT-REPAIR prompt, less so as a physics rewrite. HIGH-CONFIDENCE ADOPT: WP-1+WP-3 together (collapse the two-percussion-source role-conflation by routing blunt armour-defeat through p_auth*perc_conc, simultaneously killing the dead baked geo) — this is the single best NERS win, fixing a real one-quantity-two-owners defect AND eliminating dead data; WP-4 (resolve the seize dead-ability + ability_armature.md drift — prefer RETIRE over revive, since pre-contact seizure was proven inert); WP-6 (de-sandbox the /home/claude imports — a standalone P1 that gates every sim and the Godot port). MEDIUM ADOPT: WP-2 (decategoricalise the wt='heavy' cliff via mass-linear terms — a genuine P-iii cliff, but on a non-dominant axis, so do it cheaply with one calibrated gain per site, NOT the full MoI/pommel machinery). REJECT: the reach rewrite (already parametric+grounded, regression risk), the full 5-gain K_* set (lever-pile), pommel_kg as a live primitive (WP-5: pob_frac already suffices; correct its VALUES offline if wanted, don't add a new field), the imposition gate / 'traditions must route' (fights the substrate-bias philosophy), and roster expansions (out of scope). The corpus's analytical ledger is partly STALE vs HEAD on three counts (mass/pob_frac 'DEAD', channels 'inert pending eff_cw', POB 'inert') — all three are now live; trust core.py/systems.py over the ledger. NOTHING ships without the corpus §5 re-baseline (mirror=50, tier spread, no-one-shot) — but note that gate references a tanh CAP_END that HEAD has already removed (damage is now linear-uncapped), so the no-one-shot check must be re-specified against the QUAL ceiling + WoundTracker, not CAP_END.

reconcile.corpus: Martial-morphology union (4 vols): vol1 = 12-dim primitive scheme + 20 throughlines + 6 meta-throughlines + [M]/[B]/[L] epistemic tags + viability rules; vol2 = expanded corpus + 7 new throughlines + the 9 governing-analogues thesis (analogue-as-attractor, basins, analogue-coherence viability test); vol3 = adversarial efficacy review that adds the DECISIVE 14th dimension (resistance/aliveness), the efficacy-weighting heuristic, the efficacy-first design order, and collapses everything into a FOUR-AXIS standard (coherence / epistemic status / analogue-coherence / efficacy); distilled.md = the corpus's own self-cut to three dimension-classes (efficacy-bearing skeleton / culture-framing dressing / the single decisive variable=aliveness) + four axes. The union's own final finding (vol3 F9 'taxonomic seduction', distilled §6): the 14-dim/9-analogue elaboration OUTRAN the few variables that carry weight; meaning and efficacy run on separate tracks. ENGINE CHECKED vs HEAD: tradition.py models traditions as cognitive-mode channel-vectors (visual/tactile/precommit/leverage/tempo/measure/balance, neutral=1.0) + familiarity() cross-read + an ABILITIES armature; the resolver substrate is sigma-leverage mu-shift (core.resolve / m1 soft_cap / r1 effective_ob display-only). The corpus is a MEANING morphology; this engine is an EFFICACY resolver. That is the whole reconciliation: most of the 14 dims are creative-layer, a few map to levers already present, and the single load-bearing import (aliveness) DOES NOT APPLY where every PC is a trained, fully-resisting fighter.
already_in_engine: FOUR-AXIS 'analogue-coherence' (is the art what it says it is?) — HEAD ALREADY enforces the engine analogue of this via the import-time assertions in systems.py:86-87 (GATE/GEOMETRY must be key-synchronised with WEAPONS) and tradition.py's invariant-safe discipline (neutral=1.0 -> no-op). The corpus's 'declares geometry but moves like dance' incoherence is the engine's 'declares a weapon but has no GATE/GEOMETRY row'. The DISCIPLINE the corpus wants already exists as CI-style structural gates. Corpus is not stale here, it just names a discipline the engine implements differently.

Governing-analogue as FLAVOUR not foundation (vol3 §6.4 culture-last; distilled §3 'keep the concept, discard the 9-fold taxonomy as load-bearing') — HEAD ALREADY does exactly this. tradition.py docstring (lines 1-19): a tradition is a COGNITIVE MODE that 're-weights, does not add new physics'; mode='geometric'/'tactile'/'temporal-spatial'/'intentional' is recorded as a FLAVOUR string and the channel weights are the only mechanical surface. The engine independently arrived at the corpus's central correction (analogue = dressing over one shared substrate). CREDIT: strong convergence.

Efficacy-first / skeleton-before-culture generative order (vol3 §6.4, distilled §5) — HEAD ALREADY embodies this. The substrate (measure+commitment-window+contact-channel+collision physics) is the [M] biomechanical skeleton; traditions are layered on top as pure re-weights with invariant-safety (default fighter byte-identical). The engine fixes the [M] skeleton first and dresses last BY CONSTRUCTION. The ability_armature.md §1 'modulate never unlock' + §4 mandatory bottom-up([M] technique)+top-down(precedent) grounding IS the corpus's efficacy-first discipline operationalised.

Epistemic-tag honesty + source-tier weighting (vol3 F2 'tagging is not weighting', distilled §1 [M]/[B]/[L]) — HEAD ALREADY goes FURTHER than the corpus: ability_armature.md §5 carries S1-S5 source tiers, the 'selection-effect universal' (counter-prestige is European/Japanese, NOT universal), and explicitly DENIES abilities to priority-gap traditions (Polish szabla, Filipino, Chinese-technique) until an S1/S2 anchor exists. This is the corpus's F2 'weight, don't just tag' lesson applied to engine content. Note: armature §5 demotes Filipino to unanchored, yet tradition.py STILL ships a full 'filipino' channel-vector (lines 39-41) with mode='kinetic-rhythmic' — the channel-vector is the substrate (always present, fine) but the doc/data tension is worth a glance.

The 'information contest' meta-throughline-C (vol3 §3 'the most efficacy-faithful pattern' = sense-more-reveal-less) — HEAD ALREADY makes this the SPINE of resolution, not a flavour note. The visual/tactile read contest (wrapper:131-133 read_d vs read_a), feint_eval (systems:194), familiarity() degraded cross-read, and the Indes/sen steal (wrapper:147-153) ARE the duel-of-information. The corpus's single most load-bearing efficacy pattern is the part of the engine most heavily wired. Strong credit.

Yielding-overcomes-hardness drawn as LEVERAGE+skill, not mysticism (vol3 F5, distilled §2) — HEAD ALREADY models yielding strictly as [M]: leverage() lever-arm primitive (systems:137), bind_sigma leverage+tactile+strength (systems:228), displace-and-step-inside gated on a real leverage edge (wrapper:204-213). There is NO no-touch/aiki mystical redirection lever. The engine sits on the correct side of the corpus's jU/aiki efficacy line by construction.

Vital-point boundary [M] real-target / [B] death-touch (vol3 F6 'drawn once and lost') — HEAD AVOIDS the trap entirely: there is no dianxue/meridian-timed targeting subsystem to lose the boundary in. Targeting is collapsed into degree x coupling x armour physics (core.damage). The corpus worries the boundary is forgotten 'upward'; the engine never builds the [B] half, so there is nothing to forget. Credit by omission.

improvements.what: Collapse the dead 'seize' lever + its two abilities (vorschlag, sen_no_sen) and REPOINT them onto the live initiative substrate, OR delete them. This is the corpus's vol3-F2 'tagging is not weighting' lesson turned inward: two well-grounded abilities (S1/S2 German Vorschlag, S2 Japanese sen-no-sen — the JAPANESE flagship) are currently pure dead-data.
improvements.mechanism: tradition.py ABILITIES: vorschlag (line 96) and sen_no_sen (line 106) both set lever='seize'. NO engine site ever calls TR.ability_bonus(c,'seize') — confirmed: ability_bonus is invoked only for 'anti_overcommit' (wrapper:163) and 'counter_success' (wrapper:189). The pre-contact seizure that consumed 'seize' was CUT 2026-06-05 (wrapper:27, systems:243). IN: equipped ability -> resolver: nothing -> OUT: nothing. tradition.py:87 STILL claims "'seize' (+ seizure score)" is a LIVE lever 'wired at their engine sites' — false. FIX: either repoint these two to the surviving ongoing-initiative system (e.g. an additive bump to init_steal_factor or a one-time initiative grant at engagement start via a new bounded consumer, respecting clamp_initiative), or cut both abilities and the line-87 claim.
improvements.ners_impact: N: removes a dead lever (lever-pile defect — the canonical NERS-N failure: a mechanic computed/stored, never consumed). E: restores legibility (the doc says a thing is live that is inert). CHURN: as-is these abilities branch nothing — equipping vorschlag changes no outcome, a choice with no consequence violates the churn principle. Repointing makes the German/Japanese seizing identity actually alter the Vor contest.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST repointing: the pre-contact seize was cut BECAUSE it was empirically inert (ablation ~0, washed out by per-beat dynamics, wrapper:27). Re-adding a seize consumer risks re-introducing the exact washed-out mechanic Jordan already removed. So the SAFER half of the fix is the one to take first: DELETE the two seize abilities + fix the line-87 doc claim, rather than invent a new consumer the dynamics will just wash out again. Adding a consumer must clear an ablation bar or it is lever-pile by another name.

improvements.what: Resolve the TWO competing percussion sources for one physical quantity (blunt armour-defeat). This is a role-conflation / dead-data hybrid: mass+pob_frac feed p_auth for the DAMAGE path, but the OLD hand-set percussion field still drives the ARMOUR-DEFEAT path — the same weapon's percussive authority is computed two incompatible ways.
improvements.mechanism: core.p_auth(w) (core.py:34-38) derives percussion authority = min(8, 9.5*(sqrt(mass)*pob_frac)**0.30) and feeds core.damage blunt heft (core.py:99 passes p_auth(attacker.w)). But armor_defeat_sigma (systems.py:124) still reads the HAND-SET aggressor.w.get('percussion',8) for the blunt armour-defeat cap. IN: w['mass'],w['pob_frac'] -> p_auth -> damage heft; SEPARATELY w['percussion'] -> armor_defeat. The p_auth docstring claims it 'replaces the hand-set percussion' and that 'pob_frac & mass were dead inputs' — but the hand-set field is NOT dead, it is still the armour-defeat source, so the replacement is HALF DONE. A weapon whose hand-set perc disagrees with its derived p_auth (the whole roster: e.g. staff perc=4 hand-set vs derived) defeats armour on one number and damages on another. FIX: route systems.py:124 through core.p_auth(aggressor.w)/8.0 so both paths read one quantity; then the hand-set 'percussion' field becomes genuinely dead and can be deleted from all WEAPONS rows.
improvements.ners_impact: S (Smooth): one physical quantity should have one source across the two seams it touches (damage seam + armour-defeat seam); two sources is the role-conflation defect (one variable, >1 job — here, one CONCEPT, 2 variables). R: removes a latent inconsistency at the armour extreme where armour-defeat dominates. N: kills the half-dead hand-set field.
improvements.adversarial_note: Case AGAINST: p_auth was emergent-calibrated for the DAMAGE gradient; the hand-set percussion values were tuned for the ARMOUR-DEFEAT tier-list. Swapping armour-defeat onto p_auth may shift the validated armour tier-list (mace/poleaxe vs plate). So this is not a free refactor — it needs a re-sweep of the armour-defeat invariants. Lower severity precisely because the two numbers may have been kept separate ON PURPOSE to preserve two independently-validated calibrations; confirm with the sim before merging.

improvements.what: REJECT-adjacent but listed as the one genuinely tempting corpus import: a per-tradition 'aliveness/training-realism' scalar (vol3 Dimension-14, the corpus's single most efficacy-predictive variable). Assess it, then SCOPE it to the only place it can be non-redundant — NPC/mook tiers — never PC traditions.
improvements.mechanism: Candidate: add an aliveness in [dead..alive] per tradition, multiplying read/counter competence. IN: combatant.aliveness -> resolver: scales reading()/counter_success -> OUT: degraded net for 'dead-trained' fighters. BUT the engine ALREADY has the mechanically-equivalent axis: c.history (resolution_pool size, reading() experience term) + c.skill() per-axis biases + the S1-S5 source-tier gate in the armature. 'Trained against live resistance' = high history + trained skills; 'forms-only' = low history. The corpus's D14 is, for this engine, a RE-LABELLING of history/skill.
improvements.ners_impact: N FAILS for PCs: D14 as a new dimension is redundant with history/skill (lever-pile — an ADDED mechanic must itself be necessary; this one duplicates an existing axis). It only earns N where history can't already express it: distinguishing two same-history NPCs by training-realism for FLAVOUR (a drilled-but-never-sparred cult vs a pit-fighter) — i.e. as a CHURN seed for NPC variety, not a PC lever.
improvements.adversarial_note: Case AGAINST adopting at all (and the prompt's own challenge): 'does the aliveness axis even apply where every fighter is alive?' It does NOT for PCs — every PC is a resisting opponent by definition, so D14 is pinned at 'alive' for the entire playable cast and branches nothing. The corpus itself (vol3 §1 category-guard) says D14 only bites for combat-telos arts that sit at the dead end — which no PC does. Importing D14 as an engine dimension is the precise 14-dim over-elaboration the distillation warns against. Keep it as an NPC-generator FLAVOUR tag at most; do not give it a resolver consumer.

improvements.what: Consume or cut the DEAD baked geometry surface — thrust/cut/perc_conc/halfsword. This is the same dead-data pattern as I1/I2: computed at build-time, stored, never read. Tangential to the morphology corpus but surfaced by the same NERS-N lens the corpus's F2/F9 demand.
improvements.mechanism: combatant.py:51-53 bakes the full geometry dict and stores WEAPONS[w]['geo']=_b, but only ['gap'] is consumed (and gap already overrides the hand-set gap one line up). geometry.bake returns {gap,thrust,cut,perc_conc,halfsword}; grep confirms 'geo' is read at no engine site. IN: GEOMETRY params -> bake -> {4 dead coeffs}+gap -> OUT: only gap. The bake docstring says the coeffs are 'for downstream wiring' — the wiring never landed. FIX: either wire perc_conc into p_auth (it literally 'multiplies the §4 percussion authority' per its own docstring — a natural consumer that would ALSO help I2 unify percussion), wire halfsword into the hardcoded HALFSWORD_FORM table (systems:148), thrust/cut into coupling()/legibility(); or stop storing the dead dict and bake only gap.
improvements.ners_impact: N: four dead coefficients are lever-pile. Wiring perc_conc into p_auth is the elegant move (E): a beak/pick (poleaxe strike_concentration=0.85) SHOULD defeat plate better than a mace flat — one number doing more work, and it dovetails with I2's percussion unification. CHURN: live thrust/cut/perc_conc would let weapon GEOMETRY (not just head category) branch outcomes, more weapon-choice texture.
improvements.adversarial_note: Case AGAINST wiring: geometry.py's calibration contract says coeffs are 'calibrated to REPRODUCE the already-validated §4 values' — so wiring thrust/cut now would perturb a validated tier-list for marginal gain. The conservative correct move is the cheap one: bake only gap and drop the stored dict, UNLESS perc_conc is wired as part of the I2 percussion-unification (where it pays its way). Do not wire all four speculatively — that is gold-plating, the inverse of the distillation's cut-to-the-load lesson.

reject: The 14-dimension scheme as an engine data model. The corpus's OWN distillation (distilled §6, vol3 F9 'taxonomic seduction') says the 14 dims/9 analogues outran the few variables that carry weight and must be cut to three dimension-classes + one decisive variable. Importing the full scheme is the exact error the corpus diagnoses. The engine's 7 channel-weights + history/skill + weapon-axis vectors already span the efficacy-bearing subset; adding D1-D14 as fields would be maximal lever-pile (NERS-N catastrophe).

The 9 governing-analogues as a mechanical taxonomy / per-analogue rule-sets. vol2 makes them the 'generative substrate'; vol3 §3 + distilled §3 RETRACT this to 'flavour, largely efficacy-irrelevant, keep the concept discard the taxonomy.' tradition.py already keeps exactly the concept (mode= flavour string) over one shared substrate. Hard-coding geometry/dance/music/medicine/alchemy basins as engine branches would re-introduce per-tradition rule-sets — the precise thing 'modulate-never-unlock' (armature §1) forbids.

The aliveness axis (D14) as a PC resolver lever — see improvement I3. It is pinned at 'alive' for the entire playable cast (every PC resists), so it branches nothing for PCs and is redundant with history/skill. The single most efficacy-predictive corpus variable is the LEAST applicable one to a game where everyone is a trained, resisting fighter. Adopt at most as an NPC-flavour tag, never a dimension.

[B]/[L] mystical mechanics as new levers: qi/jin fa-jin issuing, no-touch/aiki redirection, meridian-timed dianxue/death-touch, qinggong lightness, kebal invulnerability, summoned astras. vol3 tags every one physically null [L] or mechanism-false [B]; the engine correctly has zero of them. These are pure setting/lore. Adding any as a resolver input would import asserted-fantasy-as-physics — the one move both the corpus and the engine's anti-fabrication discipline prohibit.

An efficacy-WEIGHTING heuristic (vol3 §6.3 material-fraction/convergence-proximity scorer) as an engine subsystem. It is a DESIGN-TIME sorting tool for inventing traditions, not a runtime resolver input. The engine's runtime equivalent (does this fighter win?) is the sim win-rate harness (fight()), which already measures efficacy empirically. A static efficacy score would be a second, inferior efficacy oracle alongside the actual simulation.

Exemplar weapon designs (flexible coupled-pendulum chain, traced-figure sword, gambit polearm). vol3 F4 establishes these are the LEAST effective and most physically marginal — 'emergence and efficacy are anti-correlated.' They are creative-layer worldbuilding, and importing the flexible/exotic weapon class would demand a whole new geometry+collision regime (the engine is rigid-weapon collision physics) for the corpus's own-admitted weakest designs.
creative_layer: Governing-analogue as the NARRATIVE skin of each tradition's existing mode= field: German='structure/architecture', Italian/Spanish='geometry', Japanese='strategy/no-mind', Filipino='dance-rhythm', Chinese='alchemy/medicine'. Pure prose colour for how a tradition is TAUGHT and DESCRIBED in-world — zero resolver impact, sits atop the channel-vector that already exists. This is the corpus's most intellectually substantial contribution (vol2 Part VIII) used exactly where vol3 says it belongs: meaning, not mechanics.

The healing-harming dual-use unity (vol2 throughline-22: the kalari master is a bonesetter, dit-da hit-medicine, kappo resuscitation) — a superb worldbuilding hook for NPC roster: a tradition's master is ALSO its physician. Narrative/quest texture, no engine surface. Maps to nothing in the resolver and should not.

Founding-charter legends as faction lore: the yielding-founder myth (the nun Ng Mui, the willow, the woman-general), the animal-witnessing origin (mantis besting cicada), lineage-as-carrier with its double edge (the 'too deadly to spar' secret that shields dogma). Pure setting material for sects/schools in the jianghu register Jordan wants — knowingly [B]/[L], gloriously fantastical ON PURPOSE (vol3 §7's 'liberating' framing).

The cultivation-arc as CHARACTER-PROGRESSION flavour (gross->subtle->empty / mushin), with vol3 F7's warning baked into the lore: a tradition whose upper 'internal' stages become a license to stop sparring is one whose masters have grown brittle — a built-in story flaw/antagonist seed. Maps loosely to history/skill growth but the 'transcendence trap' is narrative, not a mechanic.

Telos as faction identity (battlefield / duel / sport / health / spiritual / spectacle / communal) — vol1 D9. A flavour tag distinguishing a duelling school from a battlefield order from a temple-health lineage, driving WHO trains a tradition and WHY, with zero resolver consequence. Good NPC-faction generator input.

The four-axis standard itself as JORDAN'S design-discipline (not engine code): when inventing a new in-world tradition, deliberately place it on coherence / epistemic-status / analogue-coherence / efficacy so the fantastical is fantastical on purpose and the effective is effective on purpose. This is a worldbuilder's checklist, the single most valuable thing to KEEP from the corpus — applied at the writing desk, never compiled.
reconcile.verdict: Judicious reconciliation: the engine has ALREADY internalised the morphology corpus's hard-won central corrections, and in several places goes further than the corpus does. tradition.py independently arrived at 'analogue = flavour over one shared [M] substrate' (vol3 §3/§6.4), the resolver makes the corpus's single most efficacy-faithful pattern — the information contest, meta-C — its literal spine, yielding is modelled strictly as leverage+skill (correct side of the jU/aiki line), and the [B]/[L] mystical layer is correctly absent. The armature's source-tier + selection-effect discipline OUT-rigours the corpus's own F2 'tagging is not weighting.' Where the corpus is STALE vs HEAD: nothing material — it proposes disciplines the engine implements by other means (import-time structural gates = analogue-coherence; invariant-safe modulation = efficacy-first order). The ADVERSARIAL verdict on imports is hard NO to scale: the corpus's own distillation (F9, §6) proves the 14-dim/9-analogue scheme is over-elaboration; importing it is lever-pile. The single tempting import — the aliveness axis (D14, the corpus's most efficacy-predictive variable) — FAILS NERS-N for this engine specifically: every PC is a fully-resisting fighter, so D14 is pinned at 'alive' for the whole playable cast and branches nothing, and for NPCs it is redundant with the existing history/skill axes; keep it as an NPC-flavour tag at most. The corpus's REAL value to this project is the creative layer (governing-analogue skins, founding charters, dual-use lore) and the four-axis standard as Jordan's writing-desk discipline — applied to invent traditions, never compiled into the resolver. The genuine ENGINE work the audit surfaced is orthogonal to the corpus and is the NERS-N debt the corpus's lens HELPS you see: (I1) the dead 'seize' lever leaving vorschlag + the Japanese-flagship sen_no_sen inert with a doc that lies about it being live — highest severity; (I2) two competing percussion sources for one physical quantity (p_auth damage path vs hand-set armour-defeat path); (I4) the dead baked geometry surface (thrust/cut/perc_conc/halfsword). Net: credit the convergence, reject the scale, fix the inert levers.

reconcile.corpus: Engagement-psychology corpus (3 docs): combat_engagement_psychology_findings.md (descriptive, T1 sport-science + T2/T3 fight doctrine, self-corrected the "experts more foolable" inversion), combat_engagement_engine_proposal_unified.md (v1 prescriptive), and combat_engagement_engine_proposal_refined.md (post-adversarial, the live one). Self-audited to ONE proposed mechanic — 'wariness' (commit-depth-only, scaled by 1-familiarity, no-op at full familiarity) — plus a regime-contest lens (§2) and an optional §5 clinch/disengage-on-overcommit eligibility hook. Claims the engine ALREADY faithfully encodes disguise(legibility)/deception(feint)/overcommit-exploit. I verified all three against HEAD: designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/{wrapper.py,systems.py,core.py,tradition.py,config.py,combatant.py,geometry.py,ability_armature.md}.
already_in_engine: DISGUISE/TELEGRAPH — CONFIRMED FAITHFUL. systems.py:177-192 legibility() called wrapper.py:130: point=LEGIB_THRUST(0.80, hard to read), cuts/blunt=LEGIB_SWING(1.25, easy), +LEGIB_COMMIT_K*max(0,commit-3) (deeper commit = more readable), +LEGIB_LUNGE on lunge grip. It multiplies the DEFENDER's read (read_d, wrapper.py:131). The corpus's 'low-legibility strike IS the from-nowhere disguise; high-legibility = telegraph; harder wind-up announces louder' maps exactly. Credit accurate; no change earned (corpus agrees: no new term).

ACTIVE DECEPTION (FEINT) — CONFIRMED FAITHFUL AND THEN SOME. systems.py:194-207 feint_eval() called wrapper.py:120: feint_q=(reading+technique)*tempo vs def_read; read via logistic; fooled defender gets FEINT_DEBUFF(+0.30 to attacker), a defender who READS it gets FEINT_PUNISH(-0.12, a counter-edge). This is the forced-response/credibility contest the corrected literature wants — and crucially it is NOT a 'better readers more foolable' term, matching the corpus's retraction. Corpus credit is correct.

FEINT ALREADY ENCODES PART OF 'FACE 2'. The corpus defers 'Face 2' (familiarity wired into the disguise/feint system) as a hazardous future second touch-point. But systems.py:203 def_read ALREADY reads TR.eff_cw(defender,'precommit') AND there is a familiarity-style degrade pathway through the visual read. So the engine is FURTHER ALONG on the disguise-legibility axis than the corpus credits — the corpus is conservatively stale here in its OWN favour (it under-claims what HEAD does).

OVERCOMMIT-EXPLOIT — CONFIRMED FAITHFUL. wrapper.py:147-158 (read_win & commit>=4 -> Indes/sen-no-sen steal scaled by commit-DEPTH x read-MARGIN, bounded INDES_SCALE_FLOOR/CEIL) + wrapper.py:163 overcommit_exposure=COMMIT_EXPOSE_K*(commit-3)-anti_overcommit + wrapper.py:173-198 riposte path. Rests on the structural 'a committed action can't be simultaneously defended' fact, not a perceptual deficit — exactly the corpus's corrected biomechanical grounding. Faithful.

TWO-SIDED MEASURE/RANGE CONTEST — CONFIRMED. wrapper.py:66-85 approach (longer weapon stop-hits while shorter closes, approach_displace sets aside the point) + wrapper.py:217-226 reopen moments + systems.py:217-226 reopen_prob. The measure axis is emergent and two-sided as the corpus claims for findings §5.

DISPOSITION ALREADY CARRIES TEMPERAMENT/WRATH. config.py:91 DISP_COMMIT_K/DISP_COUNTER_K/DISP_INIT_K + wrapper.py:98-106 commit-skew + systems.py:62-64 disp_lean. The corpus's claim that 'wrath->overcommit is the existing commit-skew + fatigue' and that no new emotion subsystem is needed is correct against HEAD.

ATTACKER-IMPOSES-FIRST / VOR. The initiative substrate (systems.py:248-279, config.py:57-64) with bounded tanh edge + per-beat decay damper + hard cap is the 'who imposes the regime/holds the Vor' machinery; the corpus correctly does NOT propose to touch it.

improvements.what: Adopt 'wariness' as a commit-depth-only bias in the wrapper.py:98-106 disposition commit-skew, scaled by WARINESS_K*(1-familiarity(aggressor.tradition,defender.tradition)), with a HARD spread-floor so the combined disposition+wariness skew cannot collapse the {2,3,4,5} commit distribution to all-{2}.
improvements.mechanism: IN: TR.familiarity(ta,td) (tradition.py:62, already imported/used at wrapper.py:129) + disp_lean(aggressor) (systems.py:62). RESOLVER: in the abs(_ln)>=eps branch (wrapper.py:103-105) add the wariness term to _k BEFORE building the weight vector _w, then clamp each weight to a non-trivial floor (e.g. max(0.10,...)) so probability mass on deep commits never zeroes. config.py: add ONE constant WARINESS_K (Class-C). OUT: a shallower, more-recoverable commit distribution vs an unreadable tradition; byte-identical at familiarity 1.0 (factor zeroes). Owned state: none new.
improvements.ners_impact: N: marginal-pass. It is a genuinely absent behaviour (commit-caution vs an unreadable STYLE), and it folds into an existing lever rather than adding a subsystem — so it is not a fresh lever-pile entry. BUT see adversarial note: 'deepen familiarity' is oversold. R: the named compound-collapse interaction (cautious disp x low familiarity, both pushing toward {2}) is a real cliff risk the corpus itself flags but leaves UNSOLVED; the spread-floor I add is the missing R fix and must ship WITH the mechanic, not after. S: clean — no role-conflation at a seam, same commit-skew machinery. E: 'you commit more carefully against a style you can't read' is one-number-does-more-work, intuitable.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST: the corpus's headline justification — 'wariness DEEPENS the existing familiarity system rather than bolting on a parallel one' — is FALSE against HEAD. familiarity is ALREADY a 3-job variable: it degrades the visual read (wrapper.py:131), and it scales the bind tactile contest in BOTH directions (systems.py:236-237). Adding a 4th consumer (commit-depth) pushes familiarity from 'load-bearing' to 'role-conflated' — one scalar now driving read-accuracy, bind-dominance, AND commit-courage. A tuning change to FAMILIARITY_DEFAULT/ADJACENT (tradition.py:54-55) would then move three unrelated behaviours at once, an opaque coupling that violates the churn principle's 'one lever, one job'. The N case is therefore weaker than the corpus asserts: disposition (commit-caution as TEMPERAMENT) + familiarity (read-deficit, already felt as worse defence/bind) arguably already produce 'careful vs the unknown' emergently, and the marginal new churn (shallower commits specifically vs unfamiliar traditions) may be below perceptual threshold. Verdict: defensible but NOT clearly necessary; if adopted it must be logged as a deliberate 4th familiarity-consumer, and the worst-case spread test is a hard gate.

improvements.what: Do NOT ship the §5 clinch/disengage-on-overcommit hook as proposed; first build the contact axis it depends on, OR rescope §5 onto the EXISTING contact surface (the bind / reopen-moment).
improvements.mechanism: The corpus's §5 'make clinch/disengage more eligible when overcommit_exposure (wrapper.py:163) is high' presupposes a clinch/disengage mechanic. HEAD has NONE: ability_armature.md:61 and :169 explicitly list reopen/disengage as 'not yet built (concrete-mechanics queue)', and the clinch weapon field (combatant.py:14-27) is DEAD DATA (never read). The only contact axes that exist are the blade bind (wrapper.py:233-252) and the abstract closed/reopen measure-state. RESCOPE: the already-built reopen_moment (wrapper.py:217-218 fires when the SHORTER weapon commits>=4) is the natural home for 'change the regime on the opponent's overcommitment' — and it ALREADY keys off commit>=4, the same signal. A minimal real improvement: let overcommit_exposure additively raise reopen_prob (systems.py:217-226) so a longer weapon disengages-to-distance more readily the deeper the opponent overcommitted — reusing wrapper.py:163's existing value, no new mechanic.
improvements.ners_impact: N: the rescoped version reuses an existing signal and an existing emission (reopen) — pure wiring, no new lever. The corpus's ORIGINAL §5 fails N until the contact axis exists (you cannot wire to vapor). S: rescoping keeps it on the one contact axis HEAD actually models (measure/reopen), avoiding a phantom grappling seam. R: eligibility-boost (not hard gate) preserves spread, as the corpus correctly insists.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST: even the rescoped hook may be redundant churn — reopen_moment ALREADY fires on commit>=4 by the shorter weapon (wrapper.py:217-218), so 'overcommit opens the regime-shift' is PARTIALLY in HEAD already; adding an exposure-scaled probability term tunes magnitude but does not branch the path (the moment already exists or doesn't). Per the churn principle, a lever that only tunes and never branches is suspect. Keep §5 deferred until clinch/disengage is a real mechanic; do not let the corpus's confident '[INSERT @ wrapper.py:214-226]' imply it is shovel-ready — it is not.

improvements.what: Repair the doc-drift the corpus's §3 credit unknowingly rests on: the German/Japanese 'seize' abilities (vorschlag, sen_no_sen) are DEAD, yet docs call 'seize' live — which undercuts the corpus's claim that the engine faithfully encodes the German/Japanese engagement psychology it credits.
improvements.mechanism: IN: ability_bonus(c,'seize') — grep confirms ZERO callers in wrapper.py/systems.py. Pre-contact seizure was CUT (systems.py:243-246; wrapper.py:27-29 A.initiative,B.initiative=0.0). So tradition.py:96 'vorschlag' and tradition.py:106 'sen_no_sen' (the JAPANESE flagship) modulate a lever with no consumer = inert abilities. YET tradition.py:87 comment, ability_armature.md:51 (table: seize 'live'), and ability_armature.md:167 ('Live levers: seize (Vorschlag, Sen-no-sen)') all still claim live. FIX: either (a) re-home seize onto the surviving initiative system (e.g. ability_bonus(aggressor,'seize') as an initiative pre-load at engagement start, bounded by INIT_CAP), making the abilities real; or (b) demote the docs to 'inert (consumer cut 2026-06-05)'. The corpus credits §3's German/Japanese psychology as 'built and faithful' — (a) is the change that makes that credit TRUE.
improvements.ners_impact: N: option (a) restores churn to two tradition flagships that currently branch nothing (a dead choice = the canonical defect). E: a player equipping sen_no_sen and seeing no effect is exactly the opacity Jordan flags as the core pain. S: re-homing onto the existing initiative substrate keeps it on one axis.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST treating this as a corpus improvement: it is arguably OUT of the engagement-psychology corpus's scope — it's an ability-wiring/doc-drift bug, not a psychology mechanic, and was already in the standing audit. But it is load-bearing FOR the corpus's central credit claim: the proposal's whole 'the engine already encodes the psychology faithfully, so we only need wariness' argument silently assumes the Vor-seizure abilities work. They don't. So the corpus's §3 is stale-by-omission, and this is the one place the corpus's optimism about HEAD is materially wrong in a way that matters.

reject: REJECT the §2 'regime contest' as anything more than orientation. The refined doc itself downgrades it to 'a lens, not a mechanic' with 'one operational consequence' (the unbuilt clinch/disengage pair), so there is nothing to adopt mechanically. Against HEAD it is doubly empty: the contact axis it organizes does not exist (no clinch/disengage; clinch field dead). Credit the framing, adopt no code.

REJECT the §2 cross-corpus 'convergence' as evidence for any engine change. The refined doc already concedes (its own A1/A4) that the convergence is NOT independent — shared primary (George Silver) and shared synthesizer (same assistant). It is circular exactly as the audit prompt suspected. It raises confidence in nothing testable; the load-bearing content is wariness + the §5 hook, neither of which the convergence supports. Do not let it justify importing weapon morphology into a psychology layer.

REJECT (keep deferred) the v1 'wariness initiation knob' (a higher effective ACT_THRESHOLD vs unreadable opponents). The refined doc correctly cut it: familiarity is SYMMETRIC (tradition.py:62-67 returns the same value regardless of argument order for non-none pairs), so in the cross-tradition matchups it targets BOTH fighters raise their threshold at once -> mutual passivity / stalled bout (the actors=[] / 'if not actors: continue' path at wrapper.py:87-88 would simply spin). Confirmed against code: the cut is right; do not resurrect without a stall-guard.

REJECT 'Face 2' (wiring familiarity into legibility/feint_eval as attacker-tell disguise) AS A NEW TOUCH-POINT. Partly because the refined doc defers it, but also because HEAD ALREADY routes a precommit/read-degrade pathway through the feint (systems.py:203) and the visual read (wrapper.py:131) — adding a second explicit familiarity multiplier there would compound an effect already present and push familiarity to a 4th/5th job (see ENG-1 adversarial note). Over-granularity.

REJECT directional/asymmetric familiarity as an engine change here. The refined doc correctly routes it to Jordan's roster/flavour layer (who-studied-whom is content, not a mechanic). Making tradition.py familiarity directional is a data/matrix decision, not an engagement-psychology primitive.

REJECT any literal freeze/stun/skipped-turn state — the corpus rejects it too (brainstem-freeze is an inescapable-threat extrapolation, not a voluntary bout). Agreed; it would be unfun and break the bounded/graded R property.
creative_layer: Directional familiarity as ROSTER FLAVOUR, not engine math: the traveled duelist (an Almud-tier NPC who studied a style its bearer has never seen) reads-and-disguises against a provincial opponent who does neither in return — a genuine information asymmetry the symmetric tradition.py cannot express. Jordan's call: encode it as a per-NPC 'studied traditions' list that biases familiarity directionally only for named characters, leaving the default symmetric.

Rhythm deception / the Ali-Robinson 'telegraph-then-throw' (findings §1: once you've trained someone to read your tell as a feint, the truth becomes the best lie) is a beautiful EMERGENT story beat, not a lever — it's what a high-feint_streak fighter who then throws a high-legibility committed blow already produces. Surface it in the prose/narration layer (prose-writer skill), not in config.

Which traditions are 'adjacent' (tradition.py:56-60 ADJACENT set) is a worldbuilding artifact of Valoria's contact-history between regions — Jordan owns whether german/italian read each other better because of in-world trade/war, the same way the real HEMA exchange seeded it.

The 'wrath pulls you past your balance' (Silver, findings §3) is already mechanically the aggressive-disposition commit-skew + overcommit_exposure; the FLAVOUR — which NPCs fight in wrath, and what in the fiction provokes it — is Jordan's character layer over the existing math.
reconcile.verdict: The corpus is unusually honest and mostly RIGHT about HEAD: legibility (disguise/telegraph), feint_eval (deception), and the commit-depth Indes-steal + riposte (overcommit-exploit) are all built and faithful to the corrected psychology — I confirmed each at file:line. Its self-discipline (cutting the initiation knob on the verified-symmetric familiarity; deferring Face 2 and directional familiarity; downgrading the circular §2 convergence) is sound. Three reconciliations the corpus gets wrong or stale against HEAD: (1) its headline N-case for 'wariness' — that it merely 'deepens' familiarity — is false; familiarity is already a 3-job scalar (wrapper.py:131; systems.py:236-237), so wariness is a 4th-consumer role-conflation risk, making the mechanic defensible-but-not-clearly-Necessary; (2) §5 is wired to vapor — clinch/disengage is NOT built (ability_armature.md:61,169) and the clinch weapon field is dead data, so §5 must be rescoped onto the existing reopen-moment or deferred; (3) the corpus's §3 credit silently assumes the German/Japanese seize-psychology works, but vorschlag and sen_no_sen are DEAD (no ability_bonus('seize') consumer post-2026-06-05 cut) while three docs still call seize 'live' — the one place the corpus's optimism about HEAD is materially wrong, and the highest-severity finding. NERS bottom line: wariness EARNS A QUALIFIED place ONLY if it ships with the spread-floor (the unsolved R-cliff the corpus names) AND is logged as a deliberate 4th familiarity-job; otherwise disposition+familiarity already cover the behaviour and it is removable polish. The regime-contest lens and the cross-corpus convergence buy nothing mechanical. Adopt: wariness (low, gated on the floor test), §5-rescoped-or-deferred (medium), and — most importantly — fix the dead seize abilities (high), which is what would actually make the corpus's 'the engine already encodes this psychology' claim true.

reconcile.corpus: deliberation-as-game-synthesis.md — a research synthesis treating debate/trial/negotiation/oratory as rule-governed games. Read in full. Its load-bearing concepts for this audit: (1) constitution-by-rules (Searle/Rawls) — the master correspondence; (2) Hamblin's COMMITMENT STORE — a running ledger of what each party has conceded, a literal game-state altered by legal moves; (3) Goffman EXPRESSION GAMES + the information-contest frame (sense more, reveal less); (4) the magic-circle / interaction-membrane (bounded arena with suspended-and-transformed norms); (5) adjudicator-as-resolver (one impartial rule-applier across all arenas); (6) the four-senses taxonomy of 'game' (Wittgensteinian rule-practice / Suitsian inefficiency / agonistic zero-sum contest / game-theoretic strategic-interdependence) and the central thesis that COMBAT is agonistic-zero-sum (sense 3) while DELIBERATION is frequently MIXED-MOTIVE non-zero-sum (sense 4) — Schelling/Nash/Walton-Krabbe; (7) validity/truth/success = a WINNING STRATEGY (Lorenzen-Lorenz, Hintikka); (8) fallacy-as-foul (pragma-dialectics ten-rule code); (9) the master equivocation — an activity can be a game in sense 4 precisely by NOT being one in sense 3. LENS APPLIED: this is about the SOCIAL-contest engine (social_contest_v30.md, docket J-31), not personal combat directly. The honest strongest output is the SHARED-RESOLVER question across personal-combat (sigma-leverage continuous engine) + social-contest (discrete net-success pool engine). I read both engines at HEAD to ground the verdict; combat-specific mechanics were NOT forced out of the corpus.
already_in_engine: COMMITMENT STORE (Hamblin) is ALREADY the architecture of BOTH engines, independently realised. Combat: combatant.py:77 `self.initiative` (the Vor/Nach signed ledger) + `self.poise` (kuzushi) + wt.wounds — a running per-fighter commitment record that legal moves (hits init_GAIN_HIT, steals INIT_STEAL_INDES, bind dominance, overcommit loss, decay) alter; wrapper.py:222-225 even tracks `reopen_moment`/`push_avail` as fleeting move-state. Social: social_contest_v30 §2/§4 Persuasion Track (0-10), Doubt Markers, Concentration, Composure, and the HIDDEN GM LEDGER (§2 Step7, §4 Step7) are an explicit commitment store. Corpus credit, but corpus proposes nothing HEAD lacks here.

ADJUDICATOR-AS-RESOLVER (Part III.D, the single impartial rule-applier) is ALREADY the core.py design intent: core.py:1-2 docstring 'Single source for ob/degree/roll/damage. Wraps canonical r1/r8/m1 so every subsystem resolves identically.' Combat's resolver is role-blind (wrapper.py owns A/B identity; subsystems never index raw A/B — systems.py:2-3). The corpus's 'one role, many arenas' insight is realised WITHIN combat; the cross-arena extension (same resolver for social) is NOT done — see improvements.

MIXED-MOTIVE vs ZERO-SUM distinction (Part IV, the corpus's pivotal finding) is ALREADY honoured at HEAD by DIVERGENT resolver choice. Combat is zero-sum agonistic (sense 3): core.resolve is a single continuous net with one winner per beat, fight() returns +1/-1/0. Social is NOT forced onto that resolver: social_contest_v30 §4 CROSS interaction ('no direct comparison, each argument evaluated independently, no strain dealt'), the Compromise zone (Track 4-6), and 'private negotiation stall IS a consequential outcome' (§2 Step4) are exactly mixed-motive, non-zero-sum structure. The repo already DID what the corpus argues for: it did NOT reuse the zero-sum combat resolver for the mixed-motive social case.

INFORMATION-CONTEST / expression-games frame (Goffman, Part I.F) is ALREADY deep in combat: the read contest (wrapper.py:129-133 read_d/read_a, legibility, familiarity), feint as deception (systems.feint_eval), and Fuhlen/tactile bind-reading (bind_sigma). Social mirrors it: §4 Step1 Appraise (sense the audience), Read results becoming permanent knowledge. Corpus credit; nothing new proposed.

SHARED DERIVED-VALUE ARCHITECTURE across the two engines already exists and the prior NERS audit already CREDITED it: ners_verdict_social_contest.md:14 'Composure shares architecture with personal combat'. Composure = Charisma+6 ∥ Health = Endurance+6; Argue Pool = (Attr×2)+History ∥ Combat Pool = (Agi×2)+History+3; both TN 7; both use the same four-band degree ladder (fail/partial/success/overwhelming). This is the substrate-sharing the corpus's constitution-by-rules thesis predicts — and it is already partial fact, not a proposal.

CORPUS IS STALE vs HEAD on deliberation-determinism: the synthesis (and the SUPERSEDED social_contest_system_v2.md §5, ED-138) treats social first-to-speak as intentionally DETERMINISTIC (higher Attunement) precisely BECAUSE deliberation rewards perceptual depth not luck. HEAD REVERSED this — social_contest_v30.md:209 (ED-581) made first-to-speak ROLLED 'aligns with combat initiative for cross-system consistency.' So HEAD already chose cross-system consistency over the corpus's deliberation-is-different intuition at this one seam. Flag: this is a live tension the corpus would push back on (Part VI.2-3: deliberation healthiest when LEAST treated as a contest).

FALLACY-AS-FOUL (van Eemeren, Part V.10) is ALREADY present in social as rule-penalised moves: §9.6 Forced Unmask ('violence in the chamber = immediate Unmask, violent party auto-loses') is a foul-with-penalty; Doubt Markers / Obscuring-win penalise the move-shape. Combat's analogue: the riposte-on-fail / overcommit-exposure is the 'foul punished within the system.' Corpus credit; no new mechanic needed.

improvements.what: Extract a SHARED RESOLUTION SUBSTRATE module that both engines import, rather than two parallel hand-maintained copies of pool/TN/degree-band logic. Concretely: combat already routes everything through core.py wrapping r1/r8/m1; social_contest_v30 re-specifies Pool=(Attr×2)+History, TN7, and the fail/partial/success/overwhelming bands in PROSE with no shared code. Promote core.degree() + the pool/TN constants into a substrate both consume, parameterised by regime (continuous sigma-leverage for combat; discrete net-success for social).
improvements.mechanism: core.py degree()/resolution_pool() + r1.degree_of_success() are TWO copies of the same band ladder (core.py:14-24 continuous w/ ER-2 vs r1:108-117 discrete net>=k). IN: history/attr -> pool; net + ob -> band. OUT: degree. Make r1.degree_of_success the single discrete band and core.degree the single continuous (ER-2) band, both in one resolver substrate; social imports the discrete one instead of re-prosing it. This is the corpus's 'adjudicator-as-resolver, one role many arenas' (Part III.D / V.8) made literal in code.
improvements.ners_impact: S (Smooth) major: kills the cross-engine drift risk where social's prose band and combat's code band silently diverge (already happening — v30 line 209 reversed v2's determinism for 'consistency' but the resolution atoms are still uncoordinated). E (Elegant): one number/ladder doing work across both arenas beats two subsystems. N neutral (no new lever). R neutral.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST: the corpus's OWN central finding (Part IV master equivocation) is that combat (zero-sum/sense-3) and deliberation (mixed-motive/sense-4) are DIFFERENT games — so over-unifying the resolver risks dragging combat's single-winner μ-shift into the social engine where CROSS/Compromise/stall are load-bearing non-zero-sum outcomes. The share must be the DEGREE-BAND + POOL CONSTRUCTION ONLY, never the win-condition or the net-combination. If the unification reaches the resolve() level it is a NERS-S regression, not a fix. Keep the share below the resolver.

improvements.what: Make the combat initiative/Vor ledger an explicit named COMMITMENT-STORE object with a single mutation surface, instead of scattered raw clamp_initiative() writes across ~10 wrapper sites.
improvements.mechanism: Today combatant.initiative is mutated at wrapper.py:29,41,45,152-153,166,195-196,229-230,239-240 — every steal/gain/decay/loss writes the field directly through S.clamp_initiative. The corpus's Hamblin commitment-store (Part V.5) is exactly a ledger with LEGAL MOVES as the only mutators. IN: a move (hit/steal/bind/overcommit/decay). OUT: bounded new initiative. Wrap in e.g. core.Ledger.apply(move, magnitude) so every write is typed, logged, and bound-checked in ONE place — the same 'single call-site' discipline core.strike already enforces for damage (core.py:87-99).
improvements.ners_impact: R (Robust): centralising the clamp removes the risk that one of the ~10 sites forgets clamp_initiative (the bound is the CAP half of the damper<1 AND cap loop-safety requirement). S: makes combat's ledger structurally legible the way social's GM ledger is. E: the Vor becomes one inspectable object. N neutral.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST: pure refactor, no behaviour change, and the current sites ARE all already wrapped in clamp_initiative — so the bound is not actually violated today; this buys legibility/future-safety, not a bug fix. Could be churn for its own sake. Only worth it if a move-log is wanted for observability (which Jordan's transparency preference suggests it is) — otherwise defer.

improvements.what: Resolve the deliberation-determinism seam the corpus exposes: HEAD made social first-to-speak ROLLED (v30:209, ED-581) for 'consistency with combat,' but the corpus (Part VI.2-3, and v2's own ED-138 rationale) argues deliberation rewards PERCEPTUAL DEPTH, not luck — the opposite design pressure. Pick deliberately, do not let 'consistency' auto-decide.
improvements.mechanism: Not a combat-engine change — a social-engine + cross-system coherence decision. The lever is social_contest_v30 §5 first-to-speak: rolled-Attunement (current) vs deterministic-Attunement (corpus-favoured, makes the §4-Step1 Appraise non-redundant). IN: Attunement (+roll?). OUT: who declares second (information advantage). The corpus gives a principled reason to make this ONE seam intentionally INCONSISTENT with combat: combat initiative is rolled because combat rewards variance/readiness; deliberation initiative could stay deterministic because it rewards the read.
improvements.ners_impact: S (Smooth): the corpus's whole Part VI is that forcing combat-shape onto deliberation is the canonical category error; honouring it here is a seam-consistency improvement EVEN IF the two engines diverge at this one lever (consistent-with-sibling means same WHERE the regime matches, not everywhere). N: rolling here risks making Appraise partially redundant (the v2 ED-138 worry) — a near-inert-mechanic / NERS-N smell.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST: this is squarely the social engine (J-31), outside the personal-combat audit scope, and the corpus's deliberation/combat distinction is its MOST-contested strand (Part VI.3 says even the technical core is split Dutilh-Novaes vs Hodges; the determinism intuition is suggestive, not probative). HEAD's maintainer already weighed it (ED-581) and chose rolled. Re-opening needs a sim showing Appraise went inert, not a philosophy citation. Flag for the social team, do not act unilaterally.

improvements.what: Adopt the corpus's CONSTITUTIVE-vs-REGULATIVE distinction (Searle, Part I.B/V.1) as the discriminator for which dead-data to KILL vs WIRE in combat. The engine carries dead baked data (geo surface, clinch, geo thrust/cut/perc_conc/halfsword) and dead levers (seize); the corpus gives a principled test: a mechanic earns its place only if it CONSTITUTES a move the resolver consumes (X counts as Y in context C), not merely if it is computed.
improvements.mechanism: geometry.bake (geometry.py:58-72) emits {gap,thrust,cut,perc_conc,halfsword}; combatant.py:51-53 stores the full geo dict but only gap is ever read — thrust/cut/perc_conc/halfsword are computed-never-consumed (dead-data). 'clinch' weapon field (combatant.py:14-27) never consumed. seize lever has NO consumer (vorschlag/sen_no_sen ABILITIES are dead; ability_armature.md:51,106,167 still calls seize 'live' = doc drift). Constitutive test: either WIRE the baked coefficients into coupling()/armor_defeat_sigma (perc_conc already has a natural home in p_auth/blunt; thrust/cut into damage modes) so they COUNT AS something, OR delete them and the GEOMETRY inputs that feed only them. Same for seize: either give it a consumer or cut vorschlag/sen_no_sen and fix the armature.
improvements.ners_impact: N (Necessary) major: the corpus's V.1 is the exact formal statement of the churn-principle's 'suspect any inert mechanic' — a baked coefficient never consumed generates ZERO churn and is the lever-pile defect in latent form. Cutting it is pure N gain. E: fewer phantom inputs to reason about. S: the armature doc drift (seize 'live') is an S/legibility defect — fix in the same pass.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST: this finding is NOT FROM the corpus — it is pre-existing engine dead-data that the corpus merely supplies vocabulary for; crediting the corpus risks overstating its transfer. Honest framing: the corpus's constitutive/regulative cut is a clean RUBRIC for the dead-data triage that was already needed, not a new discovery. Also, thrust/cut/halfsword may be intentionally staged for the deferred '2b puncture work' (damage docstring core.py:80) — killing them could destroy queued WIP. Verify against HANDOFF/stash before deleting; prefer WIRE-or-flag over delete for the geo surface, delete-or-fix-doc for seize.

improvements.what: If/when a NEGOTIATION sub-mode is built for social (corpus Part IV; social_contest_v30 'Negotiation compromise resolution (ZOPA-style)' is flagged future work), use the corpus's win-set/ZOPA structure — NOT the Persuasion Track and NOT the combat resolver. The corpus is explicit that negotiation is mixed-motive (overlapping win-sets, disagreement point) and structurally unlike both the zero-sum track and combat.
improvements.mechanism: Lever: a future negotiation resolver. IN: each party's reservation point + offers. OUT: agreement in the ZOPA, or impasse at the disagreement point (Nash/Schelling, Part IV). v30 §12 already self-identifies 'Negotiation compromise resolution (ZOPA-style) — identified as structurally different from Persuasion Track, not designed.' The corpus supplies the exact theory (Nash bargaining, Schelling focal points, Putnam two-level win-sets) for that gap.
improvements.ners_impact: S (Smooth): prevents the wrong-engine-for-the-pool-regime defect (P-v) — bolting negotiation onto the Persuasion Track would conflate a mixed-motive bargain with a zero-sum push. N: a distinct resolver is justified ONLY because the regime is genuinely different (the corpus proves it is); this is the rare case where a new subsystem beats reusing one.
improvements.adversarial_note: Strongest case AGAINST: speculative/future — no negotiation engine exists at HEAD, so this is design-ahead, not an actionable engine change. Risk of premature lever-pile: a third social resolver (track + ZOPA + ...) may be over-engineering when 'private negotiation = exchange majority / stall' (v30 §2 Step4) may already suffice for table play. Build only when a concrete negotiation scene demands what the track cannot express; until then the corpus citation is a design note, not a task.

reject: ARGUMENT-IS-WAR / ARGUMENT-IS-GAME conceptual-metaphor framing (corpus Part II.E) — reject as an engine input. The corpus itself flags it as the weakest-grounded, empirically-contested strand (Part VI.8: McGlone & Glucksberg, Vervaeke & Kennedy), 'suggestive not probative.' It establishes cultural availability of the framing, nothing mechanical. No lever follows from it.

Importing the corpus's ZERO-SUM 'validity = winning strategy' (Lorenzen-Lorenz/Hintikka, Part II.B) into either resolver as a mechanic — reject. It is a profound result about LOGIC, but both engines already resolve via probability/degree, not via possession-of-a-winning-strategy game trees. Encoding a winning-strategy solver would be a massive new subsystem replacing a working one-number resolver — the canonical NERS-E failure (new subsystem beaten by one number doing the work). Cite as inspiration for the commitment-store framing only (I2), not as a resolver.

Forcing combat's continuous sigma-leverage μ-shift onto the social engine for 'one resolver to rule both' — reject, and this is the corpus's OWN warning. The master equivocation (Part 0 / Part IV) is that combat is sense-3 zero-sum and deliberation is frequently sense-4 mixed-motive; a single resolver that imposed a single continuous net + single winner would destroy CROSS/Compromise/stall (the non-zero-sum social outcomes). The honest verdict is a SHARED SUBSTRATE BELOW the resolver (I1), NOT a shared resolver. Reject any read of this corpus as arguing for one resolver.

Magic-circle / bounded-arena as a NEW mechanic (Part I.C/F, V.3) — reject as redundant. Both engines already ARE bounded arenas with suspended norms (combat: the engagement loop with its own beat/tempo physics; social: the contest with §9.6 Forced-Unmask membrane-breach penalty). The concept is already instantiated; adding an explicit 'magic circle' object would be ceremony with no churn. The corpus even notes (Part VI.7) the magic-circle is itself contested for overstating boundary firmness.

Suits's lusory-attitude / voluntariness as a design constraint (Part I.A, VI.6) — reject as out-of-scope for the resolver. It is a philosophy-of-games diagnostic (is the compelled defendant a player or the stake?), genuinely interesting for FICTION/framing but with no resolver lever. Belongs in creative_layer, not improvements.
creative_layer: The Vor ledger AS a commitment store readable in-fiction: Hamblin's insight (Part II.B) is that the commitment store is a record of what has been CONCEDED. Reskin the combat initiative ledger's sign-flips as a visible 'who has conceded tempo' — a narrator could read the Vor history as a transcript ('he conceded the line on the third bind') exactly as a debate's commitment store is read back. Flavour only; the ledger already exists (I2 just names it).

Adjudicator-as-character: the corpus's adjudicator (Part III.D) is the same role across court/debate/duel — the umpire, the judge, the dialectical audience. In Valoria, the SAME NPC archetype (a marshal at a judicial duel, an Inquisitor at a tribunal, a Speaker at Parliament) could span both engines as 'the one who applies the rules and declares' — a recurring figure whose impartiality (or corruption) is itself a churn-seed. Cross-references the social adjudicator-type table (v30 §2) with a combat trial-by-battle marshal.

Trial-by-battle as the literal seam between the two engines (Huizinga 'Play and War' + Pound's descent-from-combat, Part I.C/III.B): Valoria could make a judicial DUEL a scene that BEGINS in the social-contest engine (the accusation, the Persuasion Track) and, on a specific outcome, ESCALATES into the personal-combat engine — the corpus's exact thesis that the trial is sublimated combat, run backwards into a setting where the sublimation can fail. This is the corpus's strongest narrative gift: the two engines are not just siblings, they are the SAME contest at two levels of civilisation.

eris vs eristikē (Part I.G) as a Disposition flavour: the ancient distinction between good-contest and win-at-all-costs maps onto the combat disp/lean axis and the social orator who argues to resolve vs to dominate — a Belief-aligned characterisation hook (the duellist who fights for truth vs the one who fights to win) with no mechanical cost.
reconcile.verdict: The corpus does NOT argue for a shared RESOLVER across personal-combat and social-contest — it argues for the opposite, and HEAD already got that right. The synthesis's pivotal finding (Part IV master equivocation) is that combat is agonistic zero-sum (sense 3) while deliberation is frequently mixed-motive non-zero-sum (sense 4), and 'an activity can satisfy one precisely by failing the other.' At HEAD the two engines correctly DIVERGE at the resolution atom: combat resolves a single continuous sigma-leverage net to one winner per beat (core.resolve), while social_contest_v30 resolves discrete net-success margins with genuinely non-zero-sum outcomes (CROSS = no comparison/no strain, Compromise zone, productive stall). The honest transferable claim is a SHARED SUBSTRATE BELOW the resolver — the commitment-store ledger, the pool=(Attr×2)+History construction, the four-band degree ladder, the derived-value pattern (Composure=Cha+6 ∥ Health=End+6) — most of which the prior NERS audit already credited and which the engines already realise in parallel. The one concrete, high-value engine action the corpus's vocabulary sharpens is NOT new at all: it is the dead-data triage (I4, severity high) — the corpus's constitutive/regulative test (X counts as Y, or it does not earn its place) is the formal statement of the churn-principle, and it indicts the dead baked geo surface, the dead clinch field, and the dead seize lever (with its stale 'live' armature claim) as latent lever-pile. Net: credit HEAD heavily (it already avoided the trap the corpus warns of); the actionable improvements are mostly legibility/substrate-sharing (I1, I2) and the pre-existing dead-data cut (I4), plus two flag-don't-act seams in the social engine (I3 determinism, I5 ZOPA) that are out of personal-combat scope. Reject every reading that pushes one resolver onto both regimes. Adversarial note on my own output: only I4 is a true HEAD defect with churn impact; I1/I2 are NERS-S/E refactors not bug fixes; I3/I5 belong to J-31, not this audit — do not let the corpus's elegance manufacture combat-engine tasks that the code does not need."

adversarial.target_id: Weapon-physics calibration+wiring corpus (5 docs + weapon_physics.py): a bottom-up scheme that derives reach/heft/tempo/str-demand from physical primitives {mass, head_len, grip_len, pommel_kg(NEW), wclass, hilt}, retiring categorical reach='long'/wt='heavy', plus an analytical ledger mapping weapon×tradition×state-graph gaps. Audited against HEAD of designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/ (core.py, combatant.py, systems.py, wrapper.py, geometry.py, tradition.py, config.py, ability_armature.md) and the r1/r8/m1 substrate in tests/sim/v32-combat-balance/. Engine is non-runnable on this checkout (core.py:3, combatant.py:4, systems.py:4, wrapper.py:4 all sys.path.insert '/home/claude' + '/home/claude/v32'/'/home/claude/combat_engine' — paths absent here; static analysis only, itself a P1 portability finding for CI/Godot). weapon_physics.py is NOT in the engine dir (Glob confirms 7 .py files, no weapon_physics.py; its __main__ also reads /home/claude/cb/weapon_calibrated_final.json) — the corpus is a turnkey proposal, NOT applied. VERDICT IN BRIEF: the resolution spine (mu-shift sigma_N-scaled leverage core.py:31, ER-2 inline core.py:14-24) is NERS-sound and the corpus does not touch it. The corpus's BEST contribution is repairing genuine drift (two-percussion-source; dead baked geo) and removing a categorical seam (wt='heavy' as a 2-value cliff in HEFT/WEIGHT_PEN/ACT_WEIGHT/D_WT). Its WORST risk is lever-pile: adding K_REACH/K_HEFT/K_TEMPO/K_STRD/K_ACT (5 uncalibrated gains) + pommel_kg primitive where the existing pob_frac+mass+head_len already suffice for most outputs. Adopt the drift-repair and the heft/tempo decategoricalisation; reject the reach rewrite; treat pommel_kg as optional.:WP-4
adversarial.reasoning:
CLAIM IS TRUE AT HEAD — every cited fact verified in the working tree.

(1) DEAD ABILITIES. tradition.py:96 ('vorschlag') and :106 ('sen_no_sen') both carry lever='seize', op='+', value=4.0. The ONLY consumers of TR.ability_bonus/ability_factor in the engine are wrapper.py:158 ('counter_select'), :163 ('anti_overcommit'), :189 ('counter_success'). Grep for 'seize' across the engine dir returns ZERO ability_bonus(_,'seize')/ability_factor(_,'seize') call — only the ABILITIES defs, the doc, and unrelated prose ('seized Vor' comments, systems.py:218 reopen_prob docstring 'reads to seize'). So a fighter who equips vorschlag or sen_no_sen changes NOTHING: ability_bonus(c,'seize') is computable but never invoked. Pure dead weight, both directions of the bug (computed-never-consumed AND a player-facing choice with no path-branch).

(2) THE CUT IS REAL, COMPLETE, AND DELIBERATE. wrapper.py:27-29 — 'Pre-contact seizure CUT 2026-06-05 (Jordan; verified inert - ablation ~0...)'; initiatives explicitly forced even (A.initiative,B.initiative=0.0,0.0). systems.py:243-246 — 'Pre-contact seizure CUT ... seizure_score + initiative_seize removed ... INIT_SEIZE_K 0.45*tanh washed out by per-beat dynamics ... ablation ~0 outcome impact.' config.py has NO INIT_SEIZE_K/SEIZE/seizure key (grep: no matches) — the cut left no orphan config. This is an INTENT GATE, not an accident: the lever was retired from the resolver on purpose after a sim proved it inert.

(3) DOC DRIFT CONFIRMED ON BOTH SOURCES. ability_armature.md:51 marks 'seize | ... | live'; :106 '✅ vorschlag' (seize); :131 '✅ sen_no_sen (seize)'; :167 'Live levers: seize (Vorschlag, Sen-no-sen)'. tradition.py:87-88 ALSO lies in-code: 'LIVE levers this version (wired at their engine sites): seize (+ seizure score)...'.
