Four doc sites + one code-comment site all assert a wiring that the cut removed. A designer/player reading either believes Japan's flagship sen-no-sen works; it cannot fire.

NERS VERDICT: FAILS N and E as it stands today. N — two abilities + a lever are dead weight; the worst churn failure (equip-changes-nothing). E — the doc is a legibility trap (false 'live' affordance, esp. the JAPANESE flagship). Severity HIGH is correct: this is a P2 inert+drift defect, and the inertness reaches a flagship tradition's signature mechanic, so it's at the top of the P2 band — not P1 (engine resolves correctly; nothing breaks play or mirror-fairness; equipping a no-op is invariant-safe by construction) and clearly not P3.

WHAT SURVIVED THE ADVERSARIAL ATTACK (the addition's NECESSITY bar):
- Option (a) RE-POINT seize at initiative (corpus/ledger D-1 'revive seize, highest leverage'): REJECTED. This is the precise mechanic the engine already proved inert via ablation (systems.py:244 INIT_SEIZE_K 0.45*tanh, ~0 impact, washed out by INIT_GAIN_HIT 0.18/hit + decay + steals). Re-seeding initiative at engagement start (wrapper.py:29) or piping into INIT_STEAL_INDES (wrapper.py:151) re-introduces the SAME wash-out — the standing Vor system (initiative_sigma tanh-bounded + per-beat decay/steals/hit-gains) already owns and dampens that quantity. Adding seize back is lever-pile dressed as a fix: a new additive +4.0 onto a state variable that decay+clamp will erase within a beat, with a DOMINANT-answer risk (always equip the free Vor). It would also be gated on an UNRUN sim (engine non-runnable here) re-deriving non-zero impact — and the prior sim says zero.
- Option (b) RETIRE both abilities + the 'seize' lever and repair the four doc sites (md:51,106,131,167) + the tradition.py:87-88 code comment: SURVIVES as NERS-Necessary. It CONSOLIDATES (removes dead data + a false affordance) rather than adding; it is invariant-safe (deleting no-op equips changes no matchup); it fixes a real dead/broken path — exactly the addition profile that clears the skepticism bar.
- Note the corpus is, per the brief's own verdict, a TURNKEY PROPOSAL not applied (weapon_physics.py absent; its ledger D-1 advocates reviving seize). So D-1 is itself the lever-pile risk the brief flags — and WP-4's option (b) is the disciplined counter to it.
adversarial.revised:
ADOPT option (b); REJECT option (a)/ledger-D-1's 'revive seize'.

DO (drift-repair + consolidation, NERS-Necessary, invariant-safe, no sim required):
1. tradition.py — remove the two dead abilities 'vorschlag' (:96-97) and 'sen_no_sen' (:106-107), since the 'seize' lever has no resolver consumer. If a Japanese signature is desired NOW, RE-POINT sen_no_sen at an EXISTING live lever that already has documented churn — e.g. counter_success or counter_select (koryū sen-no-sen IS a simultaneous-seize counter; the resolver counter-path at wrapper.py:158/:189 already exists and is calibrated). That gives Japan a working flagship without rebuilding the cut pre-contact contest. Vorschlag (a first-strike-takes-Vor) maps least cleanly onto a surviving live lever and is the better pure-retire.
2. tradition.py:87-88 — delete 'seize (+ seizure score)' from the in-code 'LIVE levers' comment.
3. ability_armature.md — fix all four false 'live' sites: :51 (table row 'seize | ... | live' -> remove the row or mark 'retired (pre-contact seizure cut 2026-06-05)'), :106 ('✅ vorschlag' -> retire/repoint), :131 ('✅ sen_no_sen (seize)' -> repoint to counter_* or retire), :167 ('Live levers: seize (Vorschlag, Sen-no-sen)' -> drop seize). Add a one-line note pointing at wrapper.py:27 / systems.py:243 so the cut is discoverable from the doc.

DO NOT (the rejected revival):
- Do NOT re-point 'seize' at the initiative substrate (engagement-start seed at wrapper.py:29, or INIT_STEAL_INDES at :151). That rebuilds the mechanic the 2026-06-05 ablation proved inert (~0 impact, washed out by INIT_GAIN_HIT/decay/steals); the standing Vor system already owns that churn. Reviving it is lever-pile and would be gated on an unrun sim contradicting the prior result.
- ONLY revive 'seize' if a NEW consumer (NOT the cut pre-contact contest) is designed AND a fresh sim demonstrates non-zero, non-dominant outcome impact that the existing initiative_sigma does not already deliver. Absent that evidence, retire-and-repair is the correct close.

Net: this is a documentation-and-cleanup fix, not a mechanics change — it removes dead weight and a flagship-level legibility trap with zero risk to resolution, mirror-fairness, or invariants.

adversarial.reasoning:
VERIFIED AGAINST HEAD. The dead-data claim is true. combatant.py:53 writes WEAPONS[_w]['geo']=_b once at import; grep across the whole engine dir confirms ZERO read sites for ['geo'], 'perc_conc', the baked 'cut' field, baked 'thrust', or 'halfsword'. Every substring hit for thrust/cut is either the 'cut_thrust' head category, a comment/docstring, or geometry.py's own producer functions (geometry.py:37,43,68-71). The combatant.py:53 comment 'full baked surface available to modules' is a FALSE PROMISE — no module reads it. Clean NERS-N violation (orphan emission + dead-data, paid every import).

WHAT SURVIVED THE ATTACK — the perc_conc half is load-bearing, not speculative. The blunt armour-defeat cap is systems.py:124: cap=cfg['ADEF_BLUNT']*(aggressor.w.get('percussion',8)/8.0), a flat 1.3 (config.py:28) scaled ONLY by hand-set percussion/8. Mace and poleaxe BOTH carry hand-set percussion=8 (combatant.py:23,24), so they receive IDENTICAL blunt armour-defeat today — a war-pick does NOT out-defeat a flat mace vs plate. Yet the GEOMETRY table already encodes the distinction (poleaxe strike_concentration 0.85 vs mace 0.45, combatant.py:42,41), baked to perc_conc ~0.93 vs ~0.75 (percussion_concentration, geometry.py:48-51) — then discarded. That is a REAL inert weapon-choice churn loss, not a hypothetical. perc_conc is precisely the missing dimension, and it is the same physical quantity already double-sourced (the brief's two-percussion drift: core.p_auth at core.py:38 derives percussion from mass/pob_frac for the DAMAGE branch; armor_defeat_sigma at systems.py:124 reads hand-set percussion for the ARMOUR-DEFEAT branch — two sources, one quantity).

WHAT DID NOT SURVIVE — the thrust and cut wiring. (1) thrust is collinear with gap: the point/half-sword armour-defeat cap is ALREADY systems.py:126,129 cap=cfg['ADEF_POINT']*aggressor.w['gap'], where gap is baked gap_precision(pc,cs) (geometry.py:28-35). thrust_factor (geometry.py:37-41) is the same (point_concentration,cross_section) primitives minus a curvature term — wiring it is duplicate-lever pile. DELETE is correct. The underlying drift is REAL but the target massively overstates its magnitude and live-ness, and its mechanism is partly wrong. What I verified against HEAD:

So 'percussion authority' as a physical quantity already has ONE derived owner (p_auth) for damage; the hand-set field is a vestigial second reader at exactly one armour-defeat site. WP-1 correctly identifies and would close that. That much is true, and N+ in direction (one input retired, no behaviour added).

WHERE THE TARGET IS WRONG (the attack that landed): the claim 'mace & p_auth agree by luck; staff diverge' is quantitatively backwards. Computing from actual HEAD data (combatant.py:22-24): p_auth(mace mass1.2,pob0.6)=8.000 -> hits the min(8) cap; p_auth(poleaxe mass2.5,pob0.45)=8.000 -> also caps. So cap_HAND == cap_PAUTH == 1.300 EXACTLY for both mace and poleaxe (no luck — both saturate). For staff p_auth(mass1.5,pob0.05)=4.110 vs hand-set percussion=4 -> cap 0.668 vs 0.650, a delta of 0.018 sigma. The hand table and the derived value ALREADY AGREE for all three blunt weapons; there is no divergence to fix. WP-1's behavioural impact is ~0.018 sigma on staff-vs-armour and nil elsewhere. The 'staff diverge' example is fabricated — staff's hand value 4 was evidently already chosen to match its p_auth. So the role-conflation is cosmetic (a dead-ish duplicate field), not a live correctness fault. Severity 'high' is wrong; this is P3 polish (dead-field removal), not P1/P2.

WP-1 as written (replace field with bare p_auth) would lock in the no-concentration model and KEEP perc_conc dead. The cleaner move (WP-1 + WP-3 together: cap = ADEF_BLUNT*(p_auth*perc_conc)/8) revives the dead baked datum AND consolidates the field, yielding poleaxe 1.209 > mace 0.975 > staff 0.414 — physically correct ordering and one fewer dead path. S — neutral-to-positive (no seam created). E — neutral. It is NOT a lever-pile (adds nothing). It is a tidy-up, not a fix. Compliant but low-value alone.

adversarial.reasoning:
VERIFIED TRUE against HEAD, and it survives adversarial attack cleanly. The mechanism holds at every cited site:

(1) The engine ALREADY stores pob_frac per weapon (combatant.py:14-27), alongside mass and head_len/grip_len. pommel_kg appears NOWHERE in engine code (grep over the whole repo: the only HEAD hit for 'pommel-mass' is designs/proposals/weapon_physics_and_concentration_model.md:93, marked [PROPOSED]/vetoable; no pommel_kg in combatant.py, and none of K_REACH/K_HEFT/etc. exist at HEAD — corpus is unapplied, consistent with the brief).

(2) The canonical physics derivation needs only the existing three fields. combat_residuals_pob_f5_findings_2026-06-13.md:143-148 derives authority = sqrt(mass)*pob_frac, effective striking mass = mass*pob_frac^2, and the static first moment M1 = mass*pob_frac*L (L = grip_len+head_len) — all from stored fields, no pommel. weapon_physics_ners_reconciliation.md:17 fixes the semantics (pob_frac = (hand->balance)/head_len; balance = pob_frac*head_len, top-down validated against real specimens line 19) and :28-31 gives the unified MoI law MoI = mass*r_eff^2 with SWING r_eff = pob_frac*head_len. So the target's exact claim — 'MoI ~ mass*pob_frac*head_len^2 from EXISTING fields' — matches the canonical line.

(3) The LIVE consumer already proves it: core.p_auth (core.py:34-38) = min(8, 9.5*(sqrt(mass)*pob_frac)^0.30), reading mass and pob_frac directly. The wired derived authority needs no pommel; pob_frac+mass were the dead inputs and this is their consumer.

(4) The proposal doc itself sides with the target: weapon_physics_and_concentration_model.md:17 states PoB is THE primitive and the head-mass fraction f was 'an invented primitive' now retired; :33-35 use pommel only inside a forward materials->PoB composite ('iron pommel — a point mass'); :93 lists 'pommel-mass' as exactly the descriptor 'the composite model needs to compute mass/PoB from geometry.' pommel_kg is therefore a primitive for a FORWARD computation (materials -> PoB) the engine never performs — every engine output reads PoB (as pob_frac) directly. This is precisely NERS-N: an ADDED primitive that is not necessary because an existing one suffices.

WHAT SURVIVED THE ATTACK: I tried to break it three ways and could not.
 - 'Maybe pommel_kg is needed for some output pob_frac can't give.' No: every targeted output (heft via MoI, str_demand via static_moment) is a function of {mass, pob_frac, head_len, grip_len}; the canonical formulas (M1, MoI, P_auth) are all written in those terms with zero pommel term.
 - 'Maybe it is a safeguarded intent gate.' No — the proposal explicitly demotes pommel to a forward-computation helper and elevates PoB to the primitive; treating it as optional is the doc's own stance, not a contradiction of intent.
 - 'Maybe stale/already-fixed.' Partially the opposite of stale-against-it: P_auth is NOW wired (core.py:34) using pob_frac directly, which strengthens the target — the one place mass+pob_frac became live needed no pommel, empirically.

CAVEATS / NOT-CONFIRMED (the only soft spots, all in the target's secondary justification, not its core):
 - The ners_impact's 'rho=0.69 vs the existing pob_frac and 3 named residuals' and the adversarial_note's specific numbers (longsword pob_frac 0.14 -> physics 'rearward -0.09'; staff 0.05 -> 'computed forward 0.12') do NOT appear in any HEAD doc I could find (grep for pommel/0.69/rho/residual/back-solve across designs/). These appear to come from a corpus preliminary doc not present at this checkout. They are plausible and don't change the verdict, but they are unverifiable here — flagging per the anti-fabrication discipline. The HEAD docs instead frame pob_frac as the source the physics derives FROM, and openly note pob_frac is hand-assigned with only 3 blunt anchors to calibrate against (combat_residuals_pob_f5_findings_2026-06-13.md:160-161), which supports the adversarial_note's milder point (values are hand-set and improvable) without the specific rho figure.

SEVERITY: medium -> low. This is an ADDITION-rejection / 'treat as optional' recommendation, and adopting it is a no-op on the live engine (it declines to add a field that isn't there). It correctly prevents a future lever/primitive-pile, but nothing at HEAD is broken, inert, or drifting because of it — pommel_kg does not exist in the working tree. The churn/correctness stakes are low; the value is purely 'do not let an unnecessary primitive into combatant.py.' That is a P3-polish-flavored guardrail, hence low. (Compare the corpus's genuinely medium items — the two-percussion-source drift in systems.armor_defeat_sigma and the dead baked geo — which touch live state; this one does not.)
adversarial.revised:
ADOPT the target's recommendation; it is NERS-sound and verified. REJECT pommel_kg as a live weapon-vector field. The engine already carries pob_frac (combatant.py:14-27) with mass and head_len/grip_len, and every physics output the corpus wants is computable from those: authority = sqrt(mass)*pob_frac (already wired at core.py:34-38 as P_auth), static first moment M1 = mass*pob_frac*L, MoI = mass*(pob_frac*head_len)^2 (canonical law, weapon_physics_ners_reconciliation.md:28-31; M1 derivation combat_residuals_pob_f5_findings_2026-06-13.md:148). pommel_kg is a primitive only for the FORWARD materials->PoB composite (proposal :33-35, :93) that the engine never runs, so it is NERS-N-unnecessary — exactly the 'an ADDED mechanic must itself be necessary' rule.

Concretely:
 1. Do NOT add pommel_kg to combatant.WEAPONS. If a future WP-2 wants MoI/heft, write it against the existing fields: r_eff = pob_frac*head_len, MoI = mass*r_eff^2 (SWING), with the POLE/HEAD-HEAVY r_eff variants from weapon_physics_ners_reconciliation.md:28-34 — no new per-weapon number to source or maintain.
 2. Treat the pob_frac VALUES as the legitimate target for one-time data correction, NOT a reason to ship pommel_kg. The adversarial_note is right that some hand-set pob_frac may be off, and the HEAD docs concede pob_frac is hand-assigned with only 3 blunt anchors (combat_residuals_pob_f5_findings_2026-06-13.md:160-161). If desired, back-solve the physical PoB OFFLINE and overwrite the pob_frac literals in combatant.py — an edit to an existing field, leaving the live vector unchanged. Do this only behind the mandatory re-baseline gate (mirror=50, per-attribute tier, no-one-shot) per proposal :86-87 and weapon_physics_ners_reconciliation.md:111 — and note such a correction is currently a NEAR-NO-OP on outcomes, since blunt damage is tanh-saturated (mace/poleaxe identical on vs off; combat_residuals_pob_f5_findings_2026-06-13.md:185-190), so corrected pob_frac will shift authority numbers without moving win-rates until the separate continuous-transmission re-baseline lands.
 3. Drop the rho=0.69 / per-weapon residual figures from the justification UNLESS they can be reproduced — they are not in any HEAD doc on this checkout (anti-fabrication). The verdict stands without them: the case for treating pommel_kg as optional rests on the canonical formulas already being written in {mass, pob_frac, head_len}, which is verifiable at HEAD.

Undocumented as intentional: the state map (combat_engine_flow_and_state_map.md:48) records only the mechanical formula, no rationale; and the armature (ability_armature.md §2b/§2c) reserves a SEPARATE `measure` competence for "closing" (status: pending/unwired) — so tempo is currently squatting in the role the measure channel is meant to own. This is a transitional conflation, not a designed one.

MAGNITUDE CONFIRMED AND LARGER THAN CLAIMED in the live cases: at agi=4/fat=0/poise=1, weapon_tempo = sabre 3.20, longsword 1.50, dagger 3.80, mace 1.20, poleaxe 0.90 (computed from config). The tempo factor alone makes closing rates differ up to ~4x (poleaxe vs dagger) for identical footwork.

WHAT SURVIVED THE ATTACK (3 corrections to the finding):
1. The finding's OWN worked example is WRONG. longsword and sabre have EQUAL reach_base (6.40 == 6.40). On equal reach measure_gap=0 -> closed=True at engagement start (wrapper.py:26), so the APPROACH branch (and close_rate) NEVER executes for that pair. The cited "longsword closes ~40% slower than a sabre" matchup is precisely the case where the conflation is INERT. The defect is real but the illustration is misaimed.
2. The defect IS live where a SLOW weapon is the SHORTER one (close_rate is computed only for `shorter`, wrapper.py:68): e.g. mace (reach 5.45, tempo 1.20) vs rapier (7.15) — the mace closes at ~0.32x a dagger's rate purely from swing-cadence, with footwork (balance_eff) already a separate term. THAT is the genuine spurious gradient.
3. MIRROR-FAIRNESS IS NOT BROKEN: equal reach -> no approach phase -> close_rate never fires in any mirror; the invariant suite is untouched. This is why it is not P1.

There is a partial steel-man for tempo-in-closing (managing a heavy weapon while advancing into a stop-hit window is slower), but that residue is ALREADY modelled separately by the (1-displ) stop-hit term (wrapper.py:74) and approach_displace (systems.py:209) — so tempo in close_rate is redundant, not load-bearing.

SEVERITY: P1->P2. Engine runs, output is bounded/monotone, no cliff, no unbounded loop, mirrors fair. It is an undocumented role-conflation that is inert-in-mirror but injects a physically-spurious weapon-cadence->walking-speed gradient in asymmetric-reach approach. That is the canonical P2 (ambiguity/inert-in-some-paths/drift), not a play/correctness break. NERS: fails N (tempo doing a second job the measure competence should own) and E (one number, two jobs); R/S hold (bounded, no cliff).
adversarial.revised:
ACCEPT as a real P2 role-conflation (down from P1), but FIX BY CONSOLIDATION, not a new lever. Recommendation: remove weapon_tempo from close_rate (wrapper.py:68). Closing speed should be governed by the FOOTWORK/MEASURE competence that the engine already has: balance_eff (already the other factor in the line) is the agi-derived footwork term, and ability_armature §2b/§2c already reserves the `measure` channel for "closing" (currently pending/unwired). Route close_rate through balance_eff (and, when the eff_cw measure-channel sweep lands, through TR.eff_cw(shorter,'measure')) so closing speed = footwork x measure, with weapon cadence confined to its single legitimate job (the ready accumulator). Adding a brand-new 'mobility' coefficient would be a lever-pile (N/E fail) and is rejected; the right home already exists.

DO NOT introduce a heavy-weapon closing penalty to "preserve" the current behaviour — the heavy weapon is almost always the LONGER weapon (it is being closed ON, it does not close), so removing tempo from close_rate barely touches heavy-weapon owners and correctly stops penalizing the rare slow-SHORT weapon (mace) for its swing cadence on its walk-in.

GATING: this changes asymmetric-reach approach dynamics and interacts with STOPHIT_FULL_GAP/STOPHIT_CHANCE pacing, so it is CALIBRATION-SENSITIVE and must be DEFERRED behind a sim re-sweep (the substrate is non-runnable on this checkout via the dead /home/claude imports — itself a separate finding). Land only after confirming asymmetric-reach win-rates and approach-beat counts stay in band. Also correct the finding text: replace the longsword-vs-sabre example (equal reach, inert) with a slow-short vs fast-short example (mace/poleaxe vs dagger/sabre) where the conflation is actually live.

adversarial.reasoning:
CORE CLAIM SURVIVES, with corrections. Confirmed against HEAD: `c.grip` is assigned exactly ONCE — combatant.py:66 `self.grip='normal'`. A full grep of the engine dir for any write to `.grip` returns nothing; there is no `c.grip='choke'`/`'lunge'` in wrapper.py or systems.py, no event, ability, or AI path that mutates it. Every other occurrence is a READ via getattr(c,'grip','normal') (systems.py:25, 37, 191) or a comment. Therefore the grip state machine is genuinely missing its writer, and the grip=='choke' / grip=='lunge' branches in weapon_tempo (systems.py:26-27) are unreachable. CHOKE_TEMPO_PEN=0.4 and LUNGE_TEMPO_PEN=0.6 (config.py:14) are dead config values. TA-03 is REAL.

THREE CORRECTIONS that change scope and severity:

(1) The dead-data set is LARGER than TA-03 claims. The identical root cause (grip never written) also kills LEGIB_LUNGE=0.25 (config.py:16), consumed only at systems.py:191 `if getattr(aggressor,'grip','normal')=='lunge': legib += cfg['LEGIB_LUNGE']` — a THIRD dead config value, in a DIFFERENT module (legibility, not weapon_tempo). It additionally kills a dead BRANCH in close_tempo (systems.py:40): `if w.get('closes_poorly',False) and grip!='choke'` — `grip!='choke'` is permanently True, so the documented 'chokes up to offset the pole-close penalty' path is unreachable (POLE_CLOSE_PENALTY itself is NOT dead — it always fires for closes_poorly weapons; only the choke-cancel of it is dead). TA-03 under-scoped.

(2) The doc-drift sub-claim is HALF WRONG. TA-03 says grip is documented in ability_armature.md §2b as a live mechanic. FALSE — §2b (lines 33-44) is the competence/channel table and never mentions grip, choke, or lunge; its only 'stance' (line 37) is the unrelated balance/footwork competence, which is independently LIVE via stance_stability/balance_eff (systems.py:71, 65). The real drift is in CODE docstrings: weapon_tempo (systems.py:18-21,26-27) and close_tempo (systems.py:33-40) describe the grip/choke/lunge toggle as an operating mechanic 'handled at the call site' — but no call site sets it.

(3) SEVERITY is over-rated. P1 = breaks play/correctness. This breaks nothing: outputs are byte-identical to a world without the branches, the mirror stays fair, no invariant moves. All reads use a safe getattr default and combatant.py:66 always seeds 'normal', so the branches cannot KeyError, fire spuriously, or create a cliff (NERS-R holds). This is inert scaffolding + doc drift = P2, not P1.

WHAT SURVIVED THE ATTACK: I tried to read it as a safeguarded intent-gate (forward scaffolding like the equipped/ability no-op default and the eff_cw neutral=1.0). It IS that pattern — pre-wired consumers awaiting a writer — which is exactly why it's harmless (defuses P1) but does NOT defuse P2: the config values and branches are inert today and the docstrings overstate them as live.

NERS-NECESSARY (should grip be MADE live?) — REJECT as a lever-pile. The churn grip would seed is already delivered: 'lunge = reach/commit trade' duplicates the live commit system (commit 2-5, COMMIT_SIGMA, overcommit_exposure, DISP_COMMIT skew, legibility commit term); 'choke = close control trade' duplicates the live halfsword_target auto-switch (the actual 'choke up to fight close' mechanic) plus close_tempo compression and leverage. Wiring a grip writer would add a near-duplicate lever (E/N fail), not consolidate. Not gated on any sim — this is a static-reachability fact.
adversarial.revised: Accept TA-03 as a P2 dead-data + doc-drift finding (downgrade from P1; it alters no output and is safe-defaulted scaffolding, not a correctness break). Correct and widen its scope before filing: the dead-config set is {CHOKE_TEMPO_PEN (config.py:14), LUNGE_TEMPO_PEN (config.py:14), LEGIB_LUNGE (config.py:16)} plus the dead choke-offset branch at systems.py:40 — all rooted in the single never-written `c.grip` (only ever 'normal' at combatant.py:66). Drop/repair the inaccurate sub-claim that ability_armature.md §2b documents grip as live (it does not; the real drift is the weapon_tempo/close_tempo docstrings at systems.py:18-21,26-27,33-40). RECOMMENDED FIX = consolidate, do NOT wire a writer (wiring grip live is a lever-pile vs the live commit + halfsword_target systems): delete the three dead config keys, the grip branches at systems.py:26-27 and the grip!='choke' clause at systems.py:40, the grip read at systems.py:191, and self.grip at combatant.py:66; then strip the grip/choke/lunge language from the weapon_tempo and close_tempo docstrings. If a grip ability is genuinely roadmapped, instead keep the scaffold but mark it explicitly inert-pending (as the armature already does for 'pending'/'not yet built' levers) and fix the docstrings to say so — never leave it described as live.

adversarial.severity_adjust: P1 -> P3 (downgrade). The P1 "hard binary cliff / not graded recoverable" framing does not survive; only a bounded micro-discontinuity + a benign one-beat feint-accounting lag remain.
adversarial.reasoning:
Read wrapper.py, systems.py, config.py against HEAD. The central P1 claim — "no degradation between low-positive and negative stamina; a fighter at 0.001 applies zero OOB penalty" — is FALSE because it audits only the OOB term and ignores the continuous fatigue term that rides the SAME sigma channel.

(1) GRADED DEGRADATION EXISTS, ON THE SAME CHANNEL. wrapper.py:108 computes fat_a = max(0.0, 1 - stamina/max(1,stamina_max)). This is continuous and monotonic in stamina, floored only at 0 and UNCLAMPED above 1.0 (verified weapon_tempo systems.py:29 multiplies by (1 - TEMPO_FATIGUE_K*fatigue) with no upper clamp, floored at TEMPO_FLOOR=0.7). fat_a feeds handling_penalty (systems.py:59-61 = HANDLE_K*deficit + FATIGUE_HANDLE_K*fat_a, FATIGUE_HANDLE_K=0.20) which is SUBTRACTED from atk_sig at wrapper.py:140 — the exact same sigma term OOB feeds. So a fighter at stamina=0.001 is NOT penalty-free: fat_a ~= 1.0, paying ~0.20 sigma handling penalty + a heavily slowed cadence (lines 39/50-51). As stamina crosses into the negative the handling penalty and tempo loss keep climbing continuously (at stamina=-3, stamina_max~18, fat_a ~= 1.167). The claim's premise that the only stamina-dependent penalty is the binary OOB is the error.

(2) THE OOB STEP ITSELF IS REAL BUT BOUNDED, NOT A P-iii CLIFF. wrapper.py:107 latches oob = OOB(=2, config.py:34) if stamina<=0 else 0, applied as -oob*0.5 = -1.0 sigma at line 140. There IS a genuine -1.0 sigma discrete step at the stamina<=0 boundary. But it is a MU-SHIFT into core.resolve (soft_cap = 1.5*tanh(x/1.5), M_MAX=1.5 bounds total net contribution), NOT an Ob-shift — it does not touch the Ob>=1 floor and cannot produce an unbounded effect. A bounded mu-shift on a continuous-distribution roll is a probability tilt, not a "cannot act" wall, and it sits on TOP of an already-steep fatigue curve, so the realized output stays graded. It is recoverable (RECOVERY_FRAC between bouts, wrapper.py:291). The loop is bounded: hard separation at stamina<=-4 (lines 84, 270), so the "proceed several exchanges at stamina=-3" window is real but narrow (0 to -4) with monotonically worsening fatigue throughout. This is smoothness polish (P3), not a play/correctness break (P1).

(3) THE FEINT-ORDERING SUB-CLAIM IS TRUE and the only genuinely correct finding, but minor. oob is read at line 107 BEFORE the feint stamina deduct at line 123 (FEINT_STAMINA=1.0). fat_a is computed at line 108, also BEFORE the feint deduct. So a feint's stamina cost escapes BOTH the OOB check and that beat's fatigue computation. However: oob is NOT sticky — line 107 re-evaluates every beat with no carried state (confirmed: zero oob/exhaust references in systems.py), so the resulting negative stamina IS caught the very next beat. The lag is exactly one beat, magnitude <=1.0 stamina, symmetric for both fighters (mirror-fair, no invariant broken), and self-correcting. That is a P2/P3 accounting nit, not P1.

NERS / ADDITION verdict: the finding implicitly wants OOB replaced/supplemented by a stamina-graded penalty. REJECT as a lever-pile. The engine already delivers graded exhaustion through fat_a -> handling_penalty + tempo on the same sigma channel; a second stamina-graded penalty would be two levers doing one job (fails N-Necessary and E-Elegant). No new churn is generated — exhaustion already branches the path via worsening odds, slower cadence, and the -4 collapse exit. The only NERS-positive micro-improvement is a CONSOLIDATION/correctness fix to the ordering, not a new mechanic.
adversarial.revised: REJECT the P1 as stated; re-file as a single P3 polish item. Recommended action, in priority order: (a) Fix the one-beat ordering lag — move the OOB latch (wrapper.py:107) and the fat_a computation (line 108) to AFTER the feint stamina deduction (line 123), OR move the feint deduction above line 107, so a beat's full stamina spend is reflected in both the OOB gate and the fatigue term that same beat. This is a pure correctness tidy, symmetric, no balance change of note. (b) OPTIONAL smoothness: if the -1.0 sigma step at the stamina<=0 seam ever shows as a visible kink in sim sweeps, ramp OOB over a narrow band (e.g. scale by min(1, -stamina/W) just below zero) so the discrete step blends into the existing fatigue curve — but ONLY if a sim demonstrates the kink matters; do NOT add it speculatively (it would otherwise be redundant with fat_a). (c) Do NOT add any new stamina-graded penalty mechanic — that is a lever-pile over the existing fat_a curve and fails NERS-N/E. Keep OOB as the small discrete 'running on empty' kicker it already is. Net: no P1 exists; the cliff/recoverability concern is answered by the fat_a continuous degradation on the same channel + bounded mu-shift + RECOVERY_FRAC + the -4 exit.

adversarial.severity_adjust: P1 -> P3 (demote and reframe; the filed P1 loop-break is not real)
adversarial.reasoning:
Both factual sub-assertions verify against HEAD: systems.py:201 returns new_streak=0 in the no-fire branch, and wrapper.py:121 assigns feint_streak=fv['new_streak'] with no guard on fv['do']. The grep confirms feint_streak's ONLY consumer is the cap gate at systems.py:198 (feint_streak < FEINT_MAX_STREAK) — nothing else reads it.

But the design-consequence claim — "FEINT_MAX_STREAK=3 is a dead cap" and "the designed bluff-exhaustion damper does not exist" — does NOT survive the attack. Three things break it:

1. INTENT MISMATCH. config.py:21-22 specifies the cap as "capped at 3 IN A ROW." "In a row" is decisive: a consecutive-feint counter MUST reset to 0 on any beat the feint does not fire, or "in a row" would degenerate to "3 ever." The no-fire branch returning new_streak=0 (systems.py:201) is therefore the CORRECT, intended semantics, not a bug. The claim reinterprets a "consecutive" counter as if it were meant to be an accumulator and then faults the code for not accumulating.

2. THE CAP IS NOT DEAD. To reach streak=3 the same aggressor must roll do=True on three consecutive acted beats: P = FEINT_P^3 = 0.30^3 ~= 2.7%. On that path the cap DOES fire — the next beat evaluates 3 < 3 = False and blocks the 4th feint (then resets). So the cap is reachable and load-bearing on ~2.7% of feint-opening sequences, not 0%. "Dead cap" is factually overstated.

3. THE IMPUTED DAMPER IS A STRAWMAN. The spec never promises an escalating tell or a streak-scaled exhaustion damper. The debuff is FLAT (FEINT_DEBUFF=0.30 fooled / FEINT_PUNISH=0.12 read), not streak-scaled. The bluff-exhaustion damper the design actually specifies exists and is LIVE: the per-feint stamina cost FEINT_STAMINA=1.0, gated at systems.py:199 (aggressor.stamina>0) and deducted at wrapper.py:123, plus the read-punish counter-edge. The claim faults the absence of a mechanic the design never specified while ignoring the two dampers it did.

WHAT SURVIVED THE ATTACK (the real, demoted residue): feint_streak is a near-inert lever. It threads engagement-scoped state (wrapper.py:34/121) and a config knob (FEINT_MAX_STREAK) to suppress a 4th consecutive feint that occurs ~2.7% of the time, scaling/branching nothing else — a churn/NERS-N concern (a lever that only rarely gates, never branches the path meaningfully). Separately, feint_streak is a SINGLE engagement scalar, not per-fighter, so when the aggressor changes via the tempo gate (wrapper.py:87-93) or a riposte role-flip (wrapper.py:261), one fighter's feint can gate the other's — a minor role-conflation. Both are P3 polish/legibility notes, not the P1 loop break filed. No undamped/unbounded loop exists here: FEINT_P is flat, the cap bounds consecutive fires, and stamina is the monotone drain — damper present, bound present.
adversarial.revised:
REJECT as filed (severity and kind both wrong: not a P1 loop-break; the cited reset is correct "in a row" semantics, the cap is reachable at ~2.7% and fires there, and the "missing exhaustion damper" is supplied by FEINT_STAMINA + FEINT_PUNISH). Do NOT add an accumulating-streak or escalating-tell mechanic — that would be a lever-pile (NERS-E/N fail) on top of an already-working flat-debuff + stamina-cost design, and it is gated on an unrun sim to justify any new churn.

If anything is logged, file a single demoted P3 consolidation note: feint_streak is a near-inert, engagement-scoped (not per-fighter) lever whose sole job is to gate a 4th consecutive feint that occurs ~2.7% of the time. EITHER (a) accept it as a cheap, bounded anti-machine-gun guard and annotate the docstring that the streak is intentionally "consecutive, resets on no-fire" so future auditors don't re-file this; OR (b) if the engine has no need to ever block consecutive feints (sim shows triple-feints are already vanishingly rare and harmless), delete feint_streak / FEINT_MAX_STREAK entirely and let FEINT_STAMINA be the sole feint damper — a genuine consolidation that removes dead-ish state rather than adding more. Prefer (a) unless an ablation confirms (b) changes no outcome. Also worth a one-line note: make the counter per-fighter if it is ever made load-bearing, since today it conflates both aggressors' bluff-state.

adversarial.reasoning:

Both abilities carry lever='seize': vorschlag (tradition.py:96, value=4.0) and sen_no_sen (tradition.py:106, value=4.0). The ability system has exactly four consumers, all enumerated by grep: ability_factor(defender,'counter_select') wrapper.py:158, ability_bonus(aggressor,'anti_overcommit') wrapper.py:163, ability_bonus(defender,'counter_success') wrapper.py:189, and the eff_cw() channel sites. eff_cw (tradition.py:132-136) calls ability_factor(c, channel) ONLY for the seven channel names (visual/tactile/precommit/leverage/tempo/measure/balance); 'seize' is not a channel, so eff_cw can never route it either. grep 'seize' over core.py = zero matches; over substrate (tests/sim/v32-combat-balance) = zero. So NO call with lever='seize' exists anywhere in the engine or substrate. An equipped vorschlag/sen_no_sen is a silent no-op.

ROOT CAUSE confirmed deliberate-but-orphaning: systems.py:243-246 records the "Pre-contact seizure CUT 2026-06-05 (Jordan; verified inert; ablation ~0; INIT_SEIZE_K washed out by per-beat dynamics)" — the consumer the 'seize' lever targeted (seizure_score + initiative_seize) was intentionally removed, leaving the two abilities pointing at a deleted site. ADVERSARIAL PROBES THAT FAILED TO BREAK THE FINDING: (a) intent-gate? No — a deliberately parked lever would not be advertised as "live"; instead it is claimed live in FIVE doc spots (ability_armature.md:51 lever-map "**live**", :88 template, :106 "✅ vorschlag", :131 "✅ sen_no_sen (seize)", :167 "Live levers: seize (Vorschlag, Sen-no-sen)") AND in an in-CODE comment (tradition.py:87 lists 'seize' among "LIVE levers... wired at their engine sites... + seizure score"). That tradition.py:87 self-contradiction (it disagrees with the cut recorded 150 lines later in systems.py) is an ADDITIONAL, tighter evidence pointer the finding did not cite. (b) stale/already-fixed? No — the cut (2026-06-05) is the freshest signal and HEAD still has zero consumers. (c) mirror-fairness risk? None — dead data cannot bias outcomes.

It is a CHURN failure (the Japanese flagship Sen-no-sen is wholly inert -> a tradition-differentiation lever that branches nothing) and a LEGIBILITY/trust failure (docs+code comment say "live"), but not play-breaking. adversarial.revised:
CONFIRM the finding, downgrade P1 -> P2 (dead-data + doc-drift; inert, not correctness-breaking). Two cleanup paths, pick one — do NOT add a new 'seize' subsystem (that would re-introduce the lever ablated as ~0-impact on 2026-06-05; a lever-pile, E/N fail):

PATH A (recommended — honest deprecation): Remove vorschlag and sen_no_sen from ABILITIES (tradition.py:96-97, 106-107), since their target site no longer exists. Then re-home the two techniques onto LIVE levers that already deliver their churn: Vorschlag (seizing the Vor as a first-strike) maps cleanly to the existing initiative_sigma / hit-gain Vor system or to counter_success/anti_overcommit; Sen-no-sen (simultaneous seize as the opponent commits) is mechanically the Indes steal already in wrapper.py:147-153 gated on read_win & commit>=4 — so sen_no_sen should target counter_success or counter_select (its real engine surface), not a dead 'seize'. This CONSOLIDATES onto existing churn rather than adding physics.

PATH B (minimal): If the abilities are kept as placeholders, delete the 'seize' rows and stop claiming "live." Product = 0.45*1.30*1.5*1.40 = 1.2285 > 1.0, so `rng.random() < 1.2285` is deterministically True on every qualifying read_win+commit>=4 exchange (wrapper.py:147). The probabilistic SELECTION gate silently collapses to a deterministic branch — exactly the claim's headline.

WHAT THE ATTACK ADDED (claim UNDERSTATES breadth): (a) the floor is not disp=1 — disp=2 already gives p=1.092 (SATURATED); the gradient between disp=2 and disp=1 (and any tempo-channel ability stacking on eff_cw) is silently flattened. adversarial.reasoning:
The MECHANISM is real but the SEVERITY and the consequence claim are wrong. Verified against HEAD:

ARITHMETIC SURVIVES (partly). wrapper.py:32 soft=8; :35 `while beats < soft*3` = ceiling 24; :243-244 `for _ in range(3): beats+=1` has no inner ceiling check. A body entered at the top with beats=23 hits :36 `beats+=1` -> 24, then a bind running all 3 iterations without a break -> beats=27. So `soft*3+3 = 27` is reachable. The claim's headline number is correct, AND it actually understates the bound: feint at wrapper.py:123 `beats += fv['beat_cost']` (0.3) can add fractional overshoot on top, compounding up to FEINT_MAX_STREAK. So the documented "24" is a loose, not exact, upper bound.

WORKED EXAMPLE IS OFF-BY-ONE. The claim says "enters at beats=23 can exit at beats=26." Because :36 increments before the bind block, a body whose `while` check saw beats=23 enters the bind at 24 and exits at 27, not 26. To exit at 26 the `while` must have seen beats=22. Imprecise, non-load-bearing.

THE LOAD-BEARING CLAIM IS FALSE. "The ceiling is not enforced during a bind sub-loop, so a bind ... can exit ... without ever triggering the separation path." Trace what happens after beats overshoots to 27: the bind block either returns on felled (:250) or falls through; control reaches exchanges+=1 (:262) then the burst-exit gates (:270-272). bind=True means the clean-defence exit at :272 correctly does NOT fire (a bind continues the burst BY DESIGN, comments :263-269, state-map §2 G3). Control returns to the top `while beats < 24`; with beats=27 the guard is False, the loop exits, and :273 `return None` fires — which IS a separation terminal (state-map :113 SEP0 / §2 BEAT->SEP0, "return None — separation"). So separation is NOT skipped; it is reached on the very next loop check. The overshoot DELAYS the beats-exhaustion check by at most one exchange — it does not bypass it.

NO CORRECTNESS BREAK (why it is not P1). (a) The bind loop is hard-bounded to 3 iterations with its own felled/riposte breaks — no extra rolls beyond that. (b) The dominant burst terminator is `exchanges >= BURST_MAX` (=4) at :271, not the beats ceiling; beats=24 is a backstop against pathological non-termination, and in normal flow the burst caps at 4 exchanges long before beats nears 24. (c) beats strictly increases (:36,:123,:244) and the outer guard is monotone, so termination is GUARANTEED — the genuine NERS-R concern (unbounded loop) does not exist; the loop is bounded=damper<1 AND cap, just with cap 27 not 24. (d) The overshoot is symmetric in A/B (depends only on `beats`), so mirror-fairness is untouched; no sigma floor/cap/ER-2/Ob>=1 invariant is a loop counter, so none is violated. No wound is mis-applied, no roll is double-counted.

WHAT SURVIVED THE ATTACK: only the bare fact that `beats` can exceed the documented 24 by a bounded amount. Everything the P1 claim hangs on it — skipped separation, a correctness defect — collapsed: separation fires one iteration later, and no invariant or fairness property is harmed. The residue is a cosmetic imprecision in a safety-net counter and a slightly-stale doc annotation, i.e. P3.

INTENT-GATE rebuttal: there is NO guard/flag/conditional that could make 'seize' reachable under any state — it is unconditionally dead, not safeguarded. STALE rebuttal: not fixed; HEAD still defines both with the dead lever AND still advertises them live. The staleness runs the OTHER way — the docs lag the cut. FLAGSHIP rebuttal: sen_no_sen does NOT route through an alternate lever; Japanese precommit=1.35 (tradition.py:34) survives only as a TRADITIONS channel weight into feint_eval def_read (systems.py:203), a wholly separate mechanism — the equipped ability contributes zero. DOC DRIFT is real and four-fold, all false vs HEAD: ability_armature.md:51 ('seize' = **live**), :106 (Vorschlag ✅), :131 (Sen-no-sen ✅), :167 (seize under "Live levers"); plus tradition.py:87-88 comment still calls 'seize' a LIVE lever "wired at [its] engine site." NERS: fails N (a registered lever with no consumer is the canonical dead/inert defect) and fails the churn principle (presents the German + Japanese flagship seizing abilities as a meaningful equip choice whose only outcome is no-effect — a choice with a hidden dominant answer). SEVERITY: down P1->P2. P1 = breaks play/correctness; this breaks neither — the engine is invariant-safe (no-op when unequipped, ability_armature.md:14), no crash, no wrong number, no unbounded loop, no role-conflation. It is dead-data + doc-drift, the P2 band (inert/ambiguity/drift). The kind label dead-data is correct.
adversarial.revised:
Accept the finding, reclassified P2 dead-data + doc-drift. Two coordinated fixes, no new mechanics (this CONSOLIDATES, it does not lever-pile):

REJECT any urge to re-add a 'seize' resolver. The pre-contact seizure path was ablated to ~0 outcome impact (systems.py:243-246) and re-wiring it would re-introduce the washed-out mechanic the cut removed — an E/N failure. Do NOT build a new seizing subsystem.

INSTEAD, re-home the two abilities onto a lever that already has a live consumer so the German/Japanese flagships do real, bounded work without a new subsystem:
 1. Retarget vorschlag and sen_no_sen from the dead lever='seize' onto an EXISTING wired seizing/initiative surface. The cleanest live receiver is the in-fight Vor: route a '+' seize-style bonus into init_steal_factor / the Indes steal at wrapper.py:151,158 (the German tactile+leverage / Japanese tempo steal already exists there), or fold them into 'counter_select'/'counter_success' which are live (wrapper.py:158,189). Recalibrate value=4.0 — that magnitude was sized for a raw pre-tanh seizure score and is meaningless on a probability/sigma lever; pick a Class-C value bounded by the ~+8pp tanh envelope (ability_armature.md:17) and VALIDATE against the invariant suite. This is gated on the unrun sim, so land it behind a calibration check, not blind.
 2. If retargeting is deferred, then mark them inert HONESTLY rather than leaving false "live" claims: in ability_armature.md flip line 51 to "registered; INERT (consumer cut 2026-06-05)", change the ✅ at lines 106 & 131 to a pending/inert marker, and strike 'seize' from the §7 "Live levers" list (167); and fix the tradition.py:87-88 header comment that still calls 'seize' a LIVE wired lever. The doc-drift repair is mandatory and cheap regardless of which path (1 or 2) is chosen — a designer reading "live" will mis-equip.

Either way the dead lever must not persist while advertised live. Preferred order: fix the docs now (zero-risk, P2-clearing), then schedule the retarget+recalibration as the real N-restoring work behind a sim gate.

adversarial.reasoning: PARTIALLY SURVIVES, but materially overstated; severity wrong. CONFIRMED against HEAD: sen_no_sen (tradition.py:106) targets lever 'seize'; a whole-engine grep shows NO consumer calls ability_bonus(c,'seize') or ability_factor(c,'seize'). The sole seizure consumer (seizure_score/initiative_seize) was CUT 2026-06-05 and verified inert (systems.py:243-246; Jordan ablation ~0 outcome impact). So sen_no_sen IS dead (no-op when equipped) — TL-02 correctly inherits TL-01. Same defect kills german 'vorschlag' (tradition.py:96), so this is a shared orphaned-lever class, not a Japanese-only quirk. WHAT DID NOT SURVIVE: the structural sub-claim that 'Japanese has no live mechanic expressing this identity.' That is false. Japanese's flagship channel precommit=1.35 (tradition.py:34) is consumed and load-bearing at systems.py:203 (eff_cw(defender,'precommit') multiplies read-resistance in feint_eval), and its tempo=1.20 feeds live sites at wrapper.py:117 (initiative weighting), wrapper.py:158 (counter selection), systems.py:202/268/279. The finding itself concedes 'channel weights ... are wired.' By the tradition design contract (tradition.py:1-19: 'a tradition only biases HOW its fighter reads/selects'), substrate channels — NOT abilities — ARE the primary identity expression; abilities only modulate on top (tradition.py:78; ability_armature.md:1,12). So Japanese is mechanically differentiated and DOES express its sen/precommit identity; it just lacks an additional live ability modulator. The finding conflates the live substrate layer with the dead ability layer. SEVERITY: P1 is wrong. The ability contract is 'default unequipped = no change = invariant-safe' (combatant.py:68 equipped defaults []; tradition.py:117-130 default 0.0/1.0). A dead seize ability is therefore a SILENT no-op — the explicitly-designed safe-failure mode — not a play/correctness break: nothing crashes, no invariant breaks, mirror-fairness holds. The defect is (a) an inert/orphaned mechanic and (b) doc-drift: tradition.py:87, ability_armature.md:51 and :167 still assert 'seize' is 'live.' Inert-mechanic + drift is the canonical P2 profile, not P1. NOT stale (abilities/docs never updated after the 2026-06-05 cut) and NOT a safeguarded intent gate (it's an incomplete cut leaving orphans). NERS: fails N (a registered lever with zero consumer is dead weight) and fails E (docs claim a capability the code does not deliver). The churn principle is intact via the channels (precommit/tempo branch real outcomes), so the missing-churn framing of the ability is real but low-impact.
adversarial.revised: Reframe and downgrade to P2, merge with the vorschlag twin. Do NOT add a new seize mechanic: the pre-contact seizure was deliberately CUT 2026-06-05 as ~0-impact (systems.py:243-246); re-introducing it would re-pile a lever Jordan already ablated (E/N fail, gated on an unrun sim). Instead CONSOLIDATE to the cut. Pick one: (A) Re-home both orphaned seize abilities onto a LIVE lever that matches their documented technique — sen_no_sen is genuinely 'simultaneous seize as the opponent commits,' i.e. an in-tempo counter, so retarget it to lever='counter_select' or 'counter_success' (already-wired, wrapper.py:158/189) and bound the value vs the invariant suite; vorschlag (the first-strike taking the Vor) maps naturally to initiative/tempo. This gives Japanese AND German a live ability without any new physics. OR (B) If retargeting is premature, demote both to channel-pending status and fix the drift: change tradition.py:87 and ability_armature.md:51,167 to stop calling 'seize' live (it is not — its consumer was removed), matching how staerke_schwaeche/misura/atajo are already honestly marked '(channel; pending).' Either way, restate TL-02 as: 'two seize-lever abilities (sen_no_sen, vorschlag) are orphaned by the 2026-06-05 seizure cut and three doc sites falsely list seize as live; Japanese's tradition identity is otherwise expressed via its wired precommit/tempo channels.' Strip the 'no live mechanic expressing this identity' language — it is contradicted by systems.py:203 and wrapper.py:117.

adversarial.reasoning: VERIFIED TRUE against HEAD; the claim survived every attack. (1) Deadness: tradition.py:96 (vorschlag) and :106 (sen_no_sen) both set lever='seize', op='+', value=4.0. The ONLY ability consumers in the engine are wrapper.py:158 (counter_select via ability_factor), :163 (anti_overcommit via ability_bonus), :189 (counter_success via ability_bonus). A whole-repo grep finds NO ability_bonus(c,'seize') / ability_factor(c,'seize') anywhere; the substrate files (tests/sim/v32-combat-balance/ m1/r1/r8/damage_model) contain ZERO TR./ability_bonus/ability_factor references, so no hidden consumer exists there either. systems.py:243-246 documents the pre-contact seizure (seizure_score + initiative_seize) was CUT 2026-06-05 as 'verified inert' (ablation ~0 outcome impact). (2) Independent empirical proof: designs/audit/2026-06-09-personal-combat-comprehensive/comprehensive_analysis_personal_combat.md:117 reports an n=3000 paired-seed longsword-mirror sim: 'vorschlag, sen_no_sen ... all exactly 0.0pp (identical RNG trajectories: the equip is never read). Call-site census confirms: only counter_success, counter_select, anti_overcommit consumed.' So the deadness is not just static-analysis inference, it is measured. (3) Doc-drift is real and at the cited lines: tradition.py:87 ('LIVE levers this version ... seize'); ability_armature.md:167 ('Live levers: seize (Vorschlag, Sen-no-sen) ... Each shows a positive, bounded edge') — affirmatively false; plus ability_armature.md:51 (lever table marks seize 'live'), :106 and :131 (✅ checkmarks for vorschlag/sen_no_sen). ADVERSARIAL CHECKS that did NOT exonerate: (a) Not a safeguarded intent-gate — a gate is a deliberate no-op with code present; here the consumer was deleted, orphaning the lever, and the docs claim a positive edge that the sim shows is exactly 0.0. (b) Not stale/already-fixed — HEAD still carries the false 'live' claims, and the standing decision docket (2026-06-17 M3; loose_ends_register M3) flags it as still 'needs micro-call', confirming it is open. (c) Extra severity-relevant fact: sen_no_sen is the JAPANESE flagship; with vorschlag also dead, German and Japanese each lose their named seizure ability, and chinese/filipino have no abilities at all — but that is a churn/coverage concern, not the doc-drift finding per se. NERS: ners_compliant=false because the lever fails N (Necessary) — a registered lever with no consumer is the canonical dead-data / lever-pile defect — and the doc fails P-i (legible odds): it tells the player an ability confers an edge that the resolver never reads. SEVERITY: the target's P1 is too hot. kind is doc-drift; the engine remains invariant-safe (unequipped = no-op; equipped seize = silently 0pp), so nothing breaks play or correctness — equipping vorschlag/sen_no_sen just does nothing. That is the textbook P2 profile (inert mechanic + misleading doc). Prior audits independently classed it as a vetoable micro-[CANON]/[MECH] cleanup (M3), not a play-breaker. Hence P1 -> P2.
adversarial.revised: RETIRE the dead lever and repair the docs; do NOT re-point. Concretely: (a) remove the 'vorschlag' and 'sen_no_sen' entries (tradition.py:96-97, 106-107), or down-rank them to a clearly-marked '(seize lever cut 2026-06-05; not built)' candidate status mirroring how channel-pending abilities are labelled — NOT a 'live' claim; (b) delete 'seize' from the LIVE-levers comment at tradition.py:87; (c) fix ability_armature.md:167 (drop seize from 'Live levers'; remove 'Each shows a positive, bounded edge' as applied to seize), ability_armature.md:51 (status -> 'cut / not built'), and the ✅ at :106/:131. REJECT the re-point option (point vorschlag/sen_no_sen at counter_success or a new pre-contact edge): it fails NERS-N/E as a lever-pile — it would stack a third+fourth source onto the already-live counter lever for the same job, and systems.py:244 records that the pre-contact Vor edge (INIT_SEIZE_K) empirically washed out under per-beat dynamics, so resurrecting it adds churn-neutral tuning, not a branching choice. The consolidating, churn-positive fix is retire-and-document, matching the standing M3 recommendation. NOTE for follow-up (separate finding, not this one): retiring these leaves the Japanese tradition with zero live abilities and German with only indes — a tradition-coverage gap worth a deliberate design call, but that is gated on an unrun ability-design pass and should not be bundled into this doc-drift fix.

adversarial.severity_adjust: P1 -> P3 (downgrade; core claim refuted, only a residual harness-only hardcode remains)
adversarial.reasoning: REFUTED against HEAD. The finding's central claim — "all four modules insert dead /home/claude paths" — is factually false at the cited lines. (1) core.py:3 and combatant.py:4 insert os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), '../../../tests/sim/v32-combat-balance') — a RELATIVE, __file__-anchored path. I resolved it: it normalizes to tests/sim/v32-combat-balance and all three named substrate modules (m1_dice_sigma_core.py, r1_sigma_resolution.py, r8_parity_harness.py) physically exist there (Glob confirmed). So the engine DOES reference the real substrate location, contradicting the finding's own assertion "no engine module references that path." (2) systems.py:4 and wrapper.py:4 insert os.path.dirname(__file__) — the engine's own dir, to import siblings core/config/tradition/combatant; fully portable. (3) A repo-wide grep for 'home/claude' over the engine dir returns ZERO hits — no engine module contains that string at all, let alone at the four cited lines. The cited evidence is therefore the PORTABILITY FIX, not the defect; W-01 describes a prior pre-migration state (corroborated by core.py:16-20's 'never propagated to engine CODE until now' and the structural-fix docstrings) that has since been corrected. WHAT SURVIVED THE ATTACK: a single genuine /home/claude residue, but NOT where W-01 puts it — r8_parity_harness.py:244 and :253 hardcode open('/home/claude/r8_results.json','w'). This is inside def run(seed=None) (line 202), invoked only under the substrate's own __main__ guard (line 249). core.py imports r8 and calls only pure primitives (resolution_pool, roll_net_continuous, DECISIVE_OB, TN_STANDARD) — never r8.run() — so importing the engine NEVER reaches that write. It cannot make the engine 'non-runnable on this checkout, CI, or Godot' as claimed; at worst it breaks running r8 as a standalone __main__ on non-sandbox filesystems. Severity: not P1 (no import-time breakage, engine import chain is clean and portable), reduce to P3 polish on a substrate test harness. NERS: the portable __file__-anchored insertion is the Necessary, consolidated fix (one path expression, both anchor points) — no lever-pile, no added subsystem; it is exactly the 'one fix in the right place' the rubric rewards. No mirror-fairness/invariant implications. Independent residual caveat (outside W-01's scope, do not over-credit it to this finding): the engine being import-runnable does not mean it executes — that depends on the substrate API surface, which W-01 does not test.
adversarial.revised: REJECT W-01 as written — it is stale/refuted, not a live P1. Do NOT act on the claim that the four engine modules carry dead /home/claude paths; they do not (grep: zero hits in the engine dir), and core.py:3 / combatant.py:4 already use a portable __file__-anchored relative path that resolves to the existing tests/sim/v32-combat-balance substrate. Close W-01 as fixed-before-audit. Split off the ONE real residual as a separate P3, scoped correctly: r8_parity_harness.py:244,253 hardcode open('/home/claude/r8_results.json'); replace with a path relative to __file__ (or tempfile/CWD) so the harness's __main__ entrypoint runs off-sandbox. This does not affect engine import/runnability (the write is unreachable from the engine's import path), so it is polish, not a blocker. If a true 'engine non-runnable' finding is wanted, retarget it at the real risk — substrate API drift (whether r8/r1/m1 still expose roll_net_continuous, resolution_pool, effective_ob, DECISIVE_OB, TN_STANDARD with the signatures core.py expects) — which W-01 neither tests nor establishes.
